item.title: 本周你的胜利是什么？ | What was your win this week?
item.description:
发表于嘿哟！ 希望大家周五过得愉快，周末也过得愉快！

回顾过去的一周，您为完成什么事情感到自豪？

所有的胜利都很重要 — 无论大小 🎉

开始一个新项目

item.title: 开发人员应该在下班后每周学习 20 小时吗？ | Should developers study 20 hours a week after work?
item.description:
杰西卡威尔金斯

发表于我刚刚开始阅读Robert C. Martin 的 Clean Coder并且一直很喜欢它。 但我确实遇到了一个我觉得很有趣的部分，人们可能会对它有想法。 在第一章中，有一个标记为“职业道德”的部分，他在其中谈到了开发人员如何在工作之外不断提高技能的责任。 这对我来说完全有意义，因为这是一个技术总是在变化的行业，你必须不断学习以保持相关性。

我发现有趣的部分在这句话中：

您应该计划每周工作 60 小时。 前 40 个是给您的雇主的。 剩下的 20 个给你。 在这剩下的 20 个小时里，您应该阅读、练习、学习，并以其他方式提升您的职业生涯。

然后，他继续分解数学公式，得出您有多少小时的空闲时间。

算一算。 一周有168小时。 给你的雇主 40，你的职业再给 20。这样就剩下 108。睡眠再给 56，其他一切就剩下 52。

在这篇文章中，我想对此进行一些探讨，并留下我对工作之余应该学习多少的想法。

下班后 20 小时的学习对忙碌的家庭生活有何影响？

我是一个没有孩子的单身女性，在学习方面，我的时间是我自己的，不与任何人分享。 但我很好奇这 20 个小时的学习对于那些有孩子和忙碌家庭生活的人来说是如何工作的。 在小联盟、乐队练习和孩子们参与的数百万其他活动之间，时间都去哪儿了？

也许严格的每周 20 小时不可行？ 也许最好选择适合您家庭生活的学习计划。 或者，也许您正在阅读本文并认为 20 小时是完全可行的。

如果您是家庭生活忙碌的开发者，我很想听听您对此的看法。 初级开发人员应该在下班后花 20 个小时或更多时间学习吗？

作为一名大三学生，我觉得我们中的很多人都会在下班后花时间学习，这样我们才能继续学习和成长。 但有时这可能会导致我们花更多的时间学习和精疲力竭。 当我刚开始工作时，我在下班后投入了 20 多个小时，因为我觉得我不知道的东西太多了。 回想起来，我想知道我是否做得太过分了。

大三学生每周工作 20 小时吗？ 应该更多吗？

还是应该取决于他们的个人日程安排？

很想听听你的想法😃

每周 20 小时如何适用于经验丰富的开发人员？

如果你在这个行业里比较老练，你工作之余会花多少时间学习？ 您是在从事个人项目、读书、听播客还是在做其他事情？

我很想知道经验丰富的开发人员下班后的学习是什么样的😃

结论

我觉得我提出的问题比我在本文中提供的答案还多，但这是我们社区中的一个很好的讨论。

开发人员的持续学习是什么样的？ 应该有严格的时间承诺还是应该更灵活？

在您的职业生涯中，是否会投入更多时间在工作之外学习？

很高兴听到您对我在下面的评论中提出的任何问题的想法😃

一旦暂停，codergirl1991 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，codergirl1991 将可以再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，codergirl1991 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 codergirl1991 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 codergirl1991：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 codergirl1991 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Jessica Wilkins

Posted on Nov 25

I just started reading Robert C. Martin's Clean Coder and have been enjoying it so far. But I did run across one section that I found interesting where people would probably have thoughts about it. In the first chapter, there is a section labelled Work Ethic where he talks about how it is the responsibility of the developer to continually grow their skills outside of work. That makes total sense to me because this is an industry where technology is always changing and you have to keep learning to stay relevant.

The part I found interesting was in this quote here:

You should plan on working 60 hours per week. The first 40 are for your employer. The remaining 20 are for you. During this remaining 20 hours you should be reading, practicing, learning, and otherwise enhancing your career.

He then goes onto breaking down the math for how many hours that leaves you for free time.

Do the math. In a week there are 168 hours. Give your employer 40, and your career another 20. That leaves 108. Another 56 for sleep leaves 52 for everything else.

In this article, I wanted to explore this a little bit and leave my thoughts on how much you should study after work.

How does 20 hours of studying after work affect those with busy family lives?

I am a single woman without kids and when it comes to studying, my time is my own and not shared with anyone else. But I am curious how this 20 hours of studying works for those with kids and busy family lives. Between little league, band practice, and the millions of other activities kids are involved with, where does the time go?

Maybe a strict 20 hours a week isn't feasible? Maybe it is better to pick a study schedule that works for your family life. Or maybe you are reading this and think that 20 hours is completely doable.

If you are developer with a busy family life, I would love to hear your thoughts on this. 😃

Should junior developers spend 20 hours or more studying after work?

As a junior, I feel like a lot of us will spend time studying after work so we can continue to learn and grow. But sometimes this could lead to us spending a lot more time studying and burning ourselves out. When I first started working I put in more than 20 hours after work because I felt like there was so much I didn't know. Looking back, I wonder if I was overdoing it.

Is 20 hours a week expected of juniors? Should it be more?

Or should it depend on their personal schedules?

Curious to hear your thoughts 😃

How does this 20 hours a week apply to more seasoned developers?

If you are more seasoned in this industry, how much time do you spend studying after work? Are you working on personal projects, reading books, listening to podcasts, or doing something else?

I am curious to hear what learning after work looks like for seasoned developers 😃

Conclusion

I feel like I have raised more questions then I have provided answers in this article, but this is a good discussion to have in our community.

What does continual learning look like for developers? Should there be a strict time commitment or should it be more fluid?

Are there seasons of your career where you will invest more time in learning outside of work?

Love to hear your thoughts on any of the questions I raised in the comments below 😃

item.title: Stellar 区块链 (XLM)：配置选项 | Stellar Blockchain (XLM): Configuration Options
item.description:
目前有五个相关的主要配置选项，它们是Account Merge 、 Manage Data 、 Set Options - Home Domain 、 Set Options - Weight, Thresholds, and Signers 和SetFlags 。

帐户合并

当您想删除 Stellar 区块链上的帐户时，将使用帐户合并操作。 要删除帐户，您必须删除所有原生 XLM 和子条目； 例如信任线、其他签名者、公开报价和数据条目。

管理数据

管理数据操作包括设置、修改或删除帐户元数据中的数据条目。 数据条目是附加到帐户的应用程序特定数据的键/值对。 每个数据条目都会将账户的最低余额增加一个基本储备——0.5 XLM。

设置选项：主域

设置选项操作启用许多不同的配置。 该域提供一个 TOML 文件，该文件显示有关帐户的 Stellar 集成的信息。 设置选项 - 权重、阈值和签名者

同样，设置选项操作启用许多不同的配置。 权重、阈值和签名者配置选项允许帐户为主密钥和其他签名者设置权重，设置操作阈值，并向帐户添加其他签名者以进行多重签名交易。 签名者的权重决定了签名者在每笔交易中的杠杆作用，主密钥的默认权重为1。 每个操作都被分类为具有低、中或高阈值 (0-255)，每个类别的默认阈值为零； 这保证了主密钥始终可以签署交易。 如果阈值设置为大于零，则主密钥加上附加签名者的权重需要大于操作阈值。 如果设置的操作阈值大于加在一起的签名权重，则可能会将自己锁定在帐户之外。

设置选项：设置标志； 设置信任线标志

同样，设置选项操作启用许多不同的配置。 可以设置四个标志： Authorized Required 、 Authorization Revocable 、Authorization Immutable_ 和Clawback Enabled 。 授权可撤销标志将使发行账户能够撤销对另一个账户的授权； 这会阻止其他账户转移或交易资产，并关闭所有未结订单。 授权不可变标志限制任何其他标志被设置，并禁止发行账户被合并。 回拨启用标志赋予发行账户取回其他账户已发行资产余额的任何部分的权利。 与已发行资产交互时，重要的是要知道它们设置了哪些标志，这样您就不会被撤销或收回的资产措手不及。

设置信任线标志操作使发行账户能够为各个信任线设置标志。 可以为此操作设置两个标志： Authorized和Authorized To Maintain Liabilities 。 授权标志给予另一个账户（信任者）完全授权，允许它自由地与发行的资产进行交易。 授权维护责任标志给予有限授权，允许其维护当前订单，但防止未来未经批准的操作发生。

结局

我计划用相关的代码示例编写相应的博客文章。 目前，我只是在学习 Stellar 区块链的基础知识，并利用 Stellar Laboratory 与测试网进行交互。

如果您想了解 Stellar 区块链，并在其中赚钱，请访问： https ://quest.stellar.org/



原文:

There are five main configuration options that are relevant as of now, and these are Account Merge, Manage Data, Set Options - Home Domain, Set Options - Weight, Thresholds, and Signers, and SetFlags.

Account Merge

An Account Merge operation is used when you want to delete an account on the Stellar blockchain. In order to delete an account, you must get rid of all your native XLM and subentries; such as trustlines, additional signers, open offers, and data entries.

Manage Data

The Manage Data operation consists of setting, modifying, or deleting data entries within an account’s metadata. Data entries are a key/value pair of application-specific data attached to an account. Each data entry increases the account’s minimum balance by one base reserve- 0.5 XLM.

Set Options: Home Domain

The Set Options operation enables many different configurations. The Home Domain configuration option allows an individual account to point to a web browser domain. The domain serves a TOML file that displays information about the account's Stellar integration. The stellar.toml file proves who owns the HTTPS domain linked to a Stellar account, and that the owner is responsible for accounts and assets linked on it.

Set Options - Weights, Thresholds, and Signers

Again, the Set Options operation enables many different configurations. The weights, thresholds, and signers configuration options allows an account to set weights for the master key and additional signers, set thresholds for operations, and add additional signers to an account for multi-signature transactions. The weight of a signer determines how much leverage the signer has in each transaction, and the default weight for the master key is one. Each operation is categorized as having either a low, medium, or high threshold (0-255), and the default threshold value for each category is zero; which guarantees that the master key can always sign the transaction. If the thresholds are set to be greater than zero, then the master key's plus additional signers weights need to be greater than the operation threshold. It's possible to lock yourself out of your account if the operation thresholds are set to be greater than the signature weights added up together.

Set Options: Set Flags; Set Trust Line Flags

Again, the Set Options operation enables many different configurations. There are four flags that can be set: Authorized Required, Authorization Revocable, Authorization Immutable_, and Clawback Enabled. The authorized required flag will require another account to be approved before it can hold the issued asset. The authorization revocable flag will enable the issuing account to revoke another account from being authorized; which prevents the other account from transferring or trading the asset, and closes any open orders. The authorization immutable flag restricts any of the other flags from being set and disables the issuing account from being mergeable. The clawback enabled flag gives the issuing account the right to get back any portion of the other account's balance of the issued asset. When interacting with issued assets, it's important to know which flags they have set so you're not caught off guard by revoked or clawbacked assets.

The Set Trust Line Flags operation enables the issuing account to set flags for individual trustlines. There are two flags that can be set for this operation: Authorized and Authorized To Maintain Liabilities. The authorized flag gives full authorization to another account (truster) allowing it to transact freely with the issued asset. The authorized to maintain liabilities flag gives limited authorization allowing it to maintain current orders, but prevents future operations from occurring without approval.

Outro

I plan on writing corresponding blog posts with relevant code examples. For now, I am simply learning the basics of the Stellar blockchain and utilizing the Stellar Laboratory in order to interact with the test net.

If you wish to learn about the Stellar blockchain, and earn while doing it, then please visit: https://quest.stellar.org/
item.author: gldnwzrd

item.title: 站在巨人的肩膀上：塑造我职业生涯的文章和资源。 | Standing on the Shoulders of Giants: the Articles and Resources that have Shaped my Career.
item.description:
我分享这份文章、视频和书籍列表，因为所涵盖的主题对任何开发人员都至关重要。 这些是我在需要时寻找答案时找到的文章； 可能会有不同或更好的作品涵盖我们在旅途中发现的相同想法，但这些是我在需要它们时发现的，它们塑造了我在软件工程方面的想法。 作为一名作家和一个想要回馈社区的人，我还发现创建这篇文章解决了两个问题：它帮助我巩固了关于这个职业的所有关键但不明显的知识，它帮助我找出了主要差距需要写在纸上的知识，虽然我当然可以提供我对所有这些关键问题的看法，但我不认为我可以做得比已经创作的更好，我真的想集中精力在高影响力的话题上几乎没有曝光。 当他们来找我时，我可能会定期更新它。

有了这个介绍，这里是列表，以及我对它们重要性的看法。

软件开发生命周期

  • 如果您还没有阅读，没有理由不阅读它。 它只有 413 个字符，可以放在 2 条推文中。 它确实有助于构建 Scrum 试图做的事情，理解敏捷宣言可以让我们看到 Scrum 的本质，这是一种合乎逻辑但有些简单的可靠构建软件的工具

    令人惊讶的是，这些反模式实际上并不是明显的反模式，这些实践至今仍在继续折磨软件项目这一事实说明了这一点。 阅读本书后，您会了解到项目的忙碌、恐慌和混乱的氛围是一种非常典型的体验。 读者也将有能力查明不可避免地导致失败的特定实践。

    • 如果您不知道 Jeff Atwood 是谁，您应该知道。 他的文章是软件与人交叉的宝库。 我们同样实践 Clipboard-Driven-Development (CDD) 的人也欠他https://stackoverflow.com/的恩情。 愤世嫉俗是的，但非常正确。

      编程

      • 面向对象编程不好

      挑衅但深思熟虑。 当您发现自己不同意该行业的神圣偶像时，这也提醒您听听您脑海中响起的警报。

      将 Java 与其他语言进行比较时的主要思想。 这篇文章已经过时了，因为它看到了所有新版本的 Java 尽其所能来提供功能特性，而不必想出一些中间类来容纳它。

      可能还想加入最小惊喜原则

      数据结构和算法训练。 除了显而易见的 Crack the Coding Interview 之外，Neetcode 还为其提供了绝对最好的资源，

      函数式编程

      • 学习 FP 和 Ramda
      • 学习哈斯克尔

      版本控制和构建管道

      我实际上并不宽恕这篇文章中的建议，就此而言作者也不再宽恕，但大多数软件行业仍然推崇这些概念，因此使其成为极其重要的参考资料



原文:

I am sharing this list of articles, videos and books as the topics covered are critical to any developer. These are the articles I found looking for answers when I needed them; there may different or better works covering the same ideas that we all find on our journey, but these were what I found when I needed them, and they have molded my ideas on software engineering. As a writer and one who wants to give back to the community, I also found that creating this post solves two problems: it helps me consolidate all of the critical but not obvious knowledge about the profession, and it helps me identify the major gaps of knowledge that needs to be penned to paper, and while I can certainly provide my take on all of these critical issues, I don't think that I can do it better than what has already been authored, and I really want to focus my efforts on high impact topics with little exposure. I'll probably periodically update it as they come to me.

With that intro out of the way, here is the list, and my thoughts on their importance.

Software Development Lifecycle

  • http://agilemanifesto.org/

No excuse not to read it if you have not done so yet. It's only 413 characters and it can fit in 2 tweets. It really helps to frame what Scrum tries to do, and understanding the Agile Manifesto makes seeing Scrum for what it is, a logical yet somewhat simple tool for reliably building software

The original Encypcopedia of SDLC antipatterns. What is striking is that these antipatterns are really not obviously antipatterns, illustrated by the fact that these practices continue to scourge software projects still today. A reading of this book brings with it an understanding that the hectic, panicked, and chaotic ambience of the project is a pretty typical experience. The reader would also possess the ability to pinpoint the particular practices that enivitably bring about the failures.

  • https://blog.codinghorror.com/we-hire-the-best-just-like-everyone-else/

If you don't know who Jeff Atwood is, you should. His articles are a treasure trove of software-people intersections. We who also practice Clipboard-Driven-Development (CDD) also owe him a debt of gratitute for https://stackoverflow.com/. 😉

Cynical yes, but quite on point.

Programming

  • Object-Oriented Programming is Bad

Provocative but well thought out. It is also a reminder to listen to that alarm going off in your head when you find yourself disagreeing with the sacred idols of the industry.

Key ideas when comparing Java to other languages. The article has aged well having watched all of the new versions of Java doing everything in their power to ship out functional features without having to conjure up some intermediary class to house it.

May want to toss in Principle of Least Suprise as well

Data structure and Algorithm training. Aside from the obvious Crack the Coding Interview , Neetcode has provided the absolute best resource for it,

Functional programming

  • Learn FP and Ramda
  • Learn Haskell

Versioning and build pipelines

I don't actually condone the suggestions in this article, nor does the author anymore for that matter, but the majority of the software industry still reveres these concepts, thus making it extremely important reference material
item.author: milandry

item.title: 通过移动应用程序获利的 5 大方法 | Top 5 ways to monetize your mobile application
item.description:
在本文中，我们将介绍 5 种不同的方法，您可以通过移动应用程序获利，这样您就可以通过做自己喜欢的事情开始赚钱。

付费应用

通过您的应用程序获利的最简单方法是在用户下载应用程序之前收取一次性费用。 通过设置应用程序的价格，可以在 Play 商店和 Appstore 上轻松完成此操作。

这种策略的最大缺点是，在用户有机会尝试您的应用程序之前，您就在用户面前竖起了一堵很高的墙。 如果不首先展示应用程序的价值，就很难将其出售给新用户。

我看到一些公司采用的另一种方法是在市场上发布 2 个单独的应用程序：一个免费应用程序和一个专业付费应用程序。 这解决了我之前提到的问题，但是，有一种更好的方法可以使用相同的方法，而无需创建 2 个单独的应用程序。

付费应用程序模型的另一种方法是免费增值模型。 在免费增值模式中，你让人们免费下载应用程序，你首先为他们提供价值，然后才向用户收费。

应用内购买（免费增值）

通过免费增值应用程序获利的策略之一是在您的应用程序中提供数字商品。 用户可以从您的应用程序中购买的这些数字商品称为应用内购买。

这种模型对于许多应用程序和游戏来说效果惊人，人们可以在其中购买数字货币、生命、物品、宝石等。

用户可以在商店中购买高级商品以换取 PokeCoins。 您可以赚取这些 PokeCoins，也可以购买它们。 在 Pokemon Go 发布后的前 2 年，他们产生了超过 2B

应用内购买还可以通过为专业用户提供额外好处来改善用户体验。

订阅

免费增值应用程序的另一种货币化策略是订阅模式。 这种模式与In-app purchases非常相似，唯一不同的是用户是按时间段收费的，可以是每周、每月、每年。

订阅最常见的用例是为专业用户提供额外的功能。

优点：

  • 稳定且可预测的月收入
  • 对用户体验影响不大
  • 可以显着提高参与度。

顾虑

  • 需要大量投资才能创造出出色的产品和无缝体验才能让用户放弃现金。

必须通过用户下载应用程序的特定平台来管理订阅和应用内购买。 对于 Android 用户，交易应通过 Google Play 处理，对于 iOS 用户，应通过 Apple Appstore 处理。 这是他们的商业模式，因为这些平台会从您的收入中抽成 (15-30%)。

要向您的应用程序添加订阅或应用程序内购买，您首先必须在 Play Market 和/或 App Store 上创建和设置它们，然后使用它们的 SDK 将它们集成到您的应用程序中。

与他们的 SDK 集成非常具有挑战性，它涉及到我们这边的大量代码。 特别是如果你正在为 ios 和 android 构建跨平台应用程序，例如使用 react native、flutter 或其他跨平台框架，管理订阅和 IAP 变得更加困难。 您必须与这两个 SDK 集成，并管理很多边缘情况，例如，如果用户从 play 市场购买 android 订阅，然后想在 ios 上使用您的应用程序，您会怎么做。

收入猫

我在我之前的 Startup Fitenium中集成了RevenueCat来管理订阅，这是最好的决定之一。 我不必为订阅集成 Apple 和 Google Play SDK，我只需将他们的 SDK 集成到RevenueCat中，该 SDK 有据可查且更易于使用。 其他一切都由RevenueCat在幕后神奇地管理，以正确处理付款、用户和分析。

作为跨每个平台的客户购买数据的单一真实来源， RevenueCat提供开箱即用的跨每个平台的收入、保留和流失的深入理解。

广告

移动应用程序最流行的货币化策略之一是在您的应用程序中投放广告。

在过去十年中，数字广告已成为热门话题。

据 eMarketer 称， 75% 的数字广告预算花在了移动应用上。 使用 Google AdMob 等广告提供商可以轻松地将广告集成到您的应用程序中。 安装并设置这些广告提供商之一后，它们将自动显示专门针对正在使用您的应用程序的用户的广告

您可以根据您的应用程序集成多种类型的广告。 横幅广告、插屏广告、原生广告、奖励广告。

优点：

  • 易于设置和开始

缺点：

  • 您需要大量用户/流量才能产生可​​观的收入
  • 会影响应用体验

赞助和伙伴关系

赞助和合作伙伴关系货币化策略是利基应用程序的不错选择。 如果您有一个非常有针对性的用户群体正在使用您的应用程序，您可以直接与在该领域工作的公司建立合作伙伴关系。

这种方法更难实现，因为你必须直接与公司打交道并谈成一笔好交易。 但是，如果您的应用程序是一个利基应用程序，例如，如果您正在构建一个应用程序来显示您所在城市的所有当地农民，您可以在那里购买新鲜产品并且每月有 10,000 名用户使用它。 您可以联系其中一些农民，并以月费作为交换，他们将显示在列表的顶部。 如果您通过广告将同一个应用程序货币化，您将赚得很少，因为您的本地应用程序不会被数百万人使用。

最佳实践

如您所见，您可以通过多种方式通过应用程序获利。 您的工作是使用最适合您应用的那个。

在大多数情况下，结合不同的货币化策略将改善用户体验并最大化您的收入。

例如，您可以拥有一个免费增值应用程序，该应用程序将向使用免费计划的用户显示广告，并提供隐藏广告并包括其他好处的订阅。 这种方法对 Duolingo 非常有效。

因此，发挥创意，测试不同的方法，但不要忘记您的用户，不惜一切代价将良好的用户体验放在金钱之上。 良好的用户体验大有帮助。



原文:

In this article, we are going to cover 5 different ways you can monetize your mobile application so you can start earning money by doing what you love.

Paid app

The easiest way to monetize your application is to charge a one-time payment before the user downloads the app. This can be easily done on the Play Store and Appstore, by setting the price of your application.

The biggest disadvantage of this strategy is that you put a very high wall in front of the user before they even had a chance to try your application. Without first showing the value of the app, it is quite hard to sell it to a new user.

Another approach I saw some companies take, is to publish 2 separate applications on market: one free application and one pro paid application. This solves the issue I was mentioning before, however, there is a better way to have the same approach, without the need to create 2 separate applications.

An alternative approach to the paid app model is the freemium model. In the freemium model, you let people download the app for free, you provide them value first, and only after that do you charge the user.

In-app purchases (freemium)

One of the strategies to monetize your freemium application is offering digital goods in your application. These digital goods that users can buy from your app are called In-app purchases.

This model works amazingly for a lot of applications and also games, where people can buy digital currencies, lives, items, gems, and so on.

A good example of a successful game that made a killing using IAPs is Pokemon Go. Users can buy premium items in the shop in exchange for PokeCoins. You can either earn these PokeCoins or you can buy them. In the first 2 years after the release of Pokemon Go, they generated over 2B

In-app purchases can also improve the user experience by providing extra benefits to pro users.

Subscription

Another monetization strategy for freemium applications is the subscription model. This model is quite similar to the In-app purchases, but the only difference is that the user is charged based on a period of time that can be every week, month, year.

The most common use-case for subscriptions is to provide extra features to the pro users.

A good example of a successful subscription model, that a lot of you know, is Tinder. Tinder offers most of its features for free, but if you want to send that super like, you gotta pay a monthly subscription fee.

Pros:

  • Stable and predictable monthly revenue
  • Little effect on the user experience
  • Can significantly drive engagement.

Concerns

  • requires a lot of investment to create a great product and a seamless experience to get users to part with cash.

Subscriptions and In-app purchases have to be managed through the specific platform from where the user downloaded the application. For android users, the transactions should be handled through Google Play, and for ios through Apple Appstore. This is their business model because these platforms take a cut of your revenue (15-30%).

To add subscriptions or in-app purchases to your application, you first have to create and set up them on the Play Market and/or App Store, and then, using their SDK, integrate them into your application.

Integrating with their SDKs is quite challenging, and it involves a lot of code from our side. Especially if you are building a cross-platform application for ios and android, for example using react native, flutter, or other cross-platform frameworks, it becomes even harder to manage subscriptions and IAPs. You have to integrate with both SDKs, and manage a lot of edge cases, for example, what will you do if a user buys a subscription on android from the play market, and then wants to use your application on ios.

RevenueCat

RevenueCat comes to help us to solve these issues. RevenueCat makes it easy to implement and manage in-app subscriptions, analyze customer data, and grow recurring revenue on iOS, Android, and the web.

I have integrated RevenueCat in my previous Startup Fitenium to manage subscriptions, and it was one of the best decisions. Instead of having to integrate both Apple and Google play SDK for subscriptions, with RevenueCat I simply integrated their SDK, which is well documented and much easier to work with. Everything else is magically managed by RevenueCat behind the scenes, to properly handle the payments, users, and analytics.

As your single source of truth for customer purchase data across every platform, RevenueCat provides deep understanding of revenue, retention, and churn across every platform out of the box.

Ads

One of the most popular monetization strategies for mobile applications is running ads in your application.

Over the last decade, digital advertising has become a hot topic.

According to eMarketer, 75% of the digital advertising budget is spent on mobile apps. The year 2021 saw more than $295 billion go to mobile ads.

Integrating ads in your application is quite easy using Ads providers like Google AdMob. After you install and set up one of these ad providers, they will automatically display ads specifically targeted to the user that is using your application

There are a lot of types of ads you can integrate based on your application. Banner Ads, Interstitial Ads, Native Ads, Reward ads.

Pros:

  • Easy to set up and get started

Cons:

  • You need a lot of users/traffic to generate significant income
  • can affect the app experience

Sponsorship and Partnerships

Sponsorships and partnerships monetization strategy is a good choice for niche applications. If you have a very targeted niche of users that is using your application, you can approach directly companies that are working in that niche for a partnership.

This method is harder to realize because you have to deal directly with companies and negotiate a good deal. However, if your app is a niche app, for example, if you are building an app that displays all local farmers in your city where you can buy fresh products and it is used by 10k users every month. You can approach some of these farmers and in exchange for a monthly fee, they will be displayed on top of the list. If you monetize the same application with ads, you will make very little because your local app won’t be used by millions of people.

Best practices

As you can see, there are a lot of ways you can monetize your application. Your job is to use the one that suits your application the best.

In most situations, combining different monetization strategies will improve user experience and maximize your revenue.

For example, you can have a freemium application that will display ads to users on the free plan, and offer a subscription that would hide the ads and include also other benefits. This approach is working very well for Duolingo.

So, get creative, test different methods but don't forget about your users and prioritize a good user experience over money at all cost. A good user experience goes a long way.

item.title: 通过 NPM 发布软件 | Software Release Via NPM
item.description:
丢失按钮

发表于对于本实验，我选择通过使用 NPM 来发布我的工具。

发布前的准备

如果根目录中没有，请创建一个 package.json 文件。
使用这个 npm init 创建新的 package.json 文件。

一个 package.json 文件：

  • 列出你的项目所依赖的包
  • 使用语义版本控制规则指定您的项目可以使用的包版本
  • 使您的构建可重现，因此更容易与其他开发人员共享
  • 确保在 package.json 文件中添加名称、版本和作者。

将 README.md 文件添加到包中：

  • 在文本编辑器的包根目录中，创建一个名为 README.md 的文件。
  • 在 README.md 文件中，添加有关您的包的有用信息。
  • 保存 README.md 文件。

发布您的版本：
在您的终端上使用npm login 。 使用您的用户名和密码登录。

  • 使用npm publish --access public创建和发布范围内的公共包。

    • 注意：更新类型版本中的每个数字都有其自己的含义：1）第一个数字表示主要版本。（将后面的两个数字重置为零）2）第二个数字表示软件中的微小变化（将最后一个数字重置为零）

    安装中

    在发布我的静态站点以将其本地安装在您的设备上后，您可以使用npm i button-ssg下载我的程序。

    反馈

    避免将反馈视为个人批评很重要。 当人们向我们提供反馈时，我们应该心存感激，因为他们实际上是在指出我们需要改进的地方。

    思绪

    这个实验室是一个受欢迎的实验室，它使我们能够为社区做出更多贡献（如果有的话）。 发布到 npm 对我来说是第一次，但这是有用的信息。 我一直想知道人们是如何发布他们的程序的，而现在我在这里，就是这样做的。

    穆罕默德·西法特·侯赛因 - 一旦暂停，lostbutton 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

    解除暂停后，lostbutton 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，lostbutton 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 lostbutton 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记丢失按钮的方法：

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 lostbutton 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    LostButton

    Posted on Nov 26

    For this lab, I have chosen to release my tool via the use of NPM.

    Preparation before publishing

    Create a package.json file if you don't have in the root directory.
    Use this npm init to create the new package.json file.

    A package.json file:

    • Lists the packages your project depends on
    • Specifies versions of a package that your project can use using semantic versioning rules
    • Makes your build reproducible, and therefore easier to share with other developers
    • Make sure to add the name, version and author in your package.json file.

    ADD a README.md file to a package:

    • In a text editor, in your package root directory, create a file called README.md.
    • In the README.md file, add useful information about your package.
    • Save the README.md file.

    Publish your release:
    -Make sure you have created the account npm and verified it.

    • Use the npm login on your terminal. Login with your username and password.
    • Use npm publish --access public to create and publish scoped public packages.

    Note: Update types Each digit in the version has it's own meaning: 1) First digit means major release.(resetting two later digit to zero) 2) Second digit means minor change in the software(resetting last digit to zero)

    3) Last digit means a patch release.

    Installing

    After releasing my static site to install it locally on your device you can use npm i button-ssg to download my program.

    Feedback

    It's important to avoid taking feedback as personal criticism. When people provide us with feedback, we should be grateful because they are actually pointing out areas in which we need to improve.

    Thoughts

    This lab was a welcome one, that allowed us to contribute more to the community if anything. It was a first for me when publishing to npm, but this is useful information. I have always wondered how people were publishing their programs and now here I am, doing just that.

    Muhammad Sifat Hossain - Nov 8

    Once suspended, lostbutton will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, lostbutton will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by lostbutton will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If lostbutton is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag lostbutton:

    lostbutton consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging lostbutton will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: lostbutton

item.title: SVG 解释 | SVGs Explained
item.description:
目录

与声音一样，这些都是可缩放的图像，非常适合响应式设计。 可扩展性很棒，因为您可以放大 SVG 图像而不会出现任何退化。 通常比 JPEG 或 PNG 小得多。 如果您不擅长绘图点，您可以使用 Adob​​e 创意套件创建一个或开源选项，如 GIMP、Inkscape 或 Krita。 其他一些很棒的用例是它们非常适合为图像创建遮罩。

由 World Wide Wed Consortium (W3C) 在 90 年代后期创建。 它的开发旨在为开发人员创建一种基于矢量的图像的方式，该图像可以扩展并允许响应式设计。 一个 SVG 示例：

 <svg viewBox= '0 0 100 100' > <circle cx= '50' cy= '50' r= '50' fill= 'red' /> </svg>

这将呈现一个红色圆圈，这是无需使用绘图应用程序即可制作的基本形状之一。 将圆圈移到viewBox之外将切掉该部分。 由于绘图是完全可缩放的，我发现使用 100 并使用它作为百分比参考来绘制其余数字。 接下来是cx和cy ，这是圆的中心 x 和中心 y 图。 然后是r ，它指的是圆的半径。 最后， fill允许您使用一些拼写颜色和任何十六进制代码颜色进行着色。

如何制作 SVG

 <svg viewBox= '0 0 100 100' > <path stroke= 'white' fill= 'none' d= " M 10 10 H 90 V 90 H 10 V 10 " /> </svg>

这是一个看起来很复杂的元素，这是一个简单的正方形白色轮廓。 绘图的结构定义了由M定义的线的种类或起点。 数字后跟x, y绘图点。 默认填充为黑色，将填充设置为none将使该区域透明。 要查找更多属性，我建议查看MDN 文档中的d属性，例如绘制圆弧和其他线/点。

高级 SVG 使用

您可以使用 SVG 的一些很酷的技巧是在您悬停时使用 CSS 中的path属性向 SVG 添加更改。 这方面的一个例子是：

索引.html

 <svg id= "svg_css" viewBox= "0 0 100 100" > <path fill= "none" stroke= "red" d= " M 100, 100 m -100, -50 a 50,50 0 1,0 100,0 a 50,50 0 1,0 -100,0 " /> </svg>

样式.css

 html , body { height : 100% ; width : 100% ; } #svg_css :hover path { d : path ( "M 10,10 H 90 V 90 H 10 V 10" ); }

这会改变对象的路径，但您可能会更改或添加任何属性的 CSS 规则，包括描边或填充颜色。

您可以做的另一件很酷的事情是屏蔽。 遮罩是用图像填充 SVG 的背景。 这种效果确实可以为您的 SVG 添加一个新的维度。

 <svg width= "600" height= "400" > <mask id= "svgmask1" > <polygon fill= "#ffffff" points= "200 0, 400 400, 0 400" /> </mask> <image xlink:href= "cat.jpeg" mask= "url(#svgmask1)" /> </svg>

这将渲染一个三角形，其中cat.jpeg被三角形遮盖了。 潜在地，您可以在一个 SVG 中添加多个形状和多个图像。 这具有真正深厚的、具有无限可能的创造力。

结论

这只是 SVG 的基础知识和使用它们来获得更深入理解的技术，您应该更深入地研究 SVG 上的MDN 文档； 还有很多东西需要深入研究。 对于您可能想要开发的更复杂的图像，我建议使用艺术应用程序，例如 Inkscape 或 Gimp，它会将您的矢量图像转换为 SVG。



原文:

Table of Contents

A SVG is..

SVG stands for scalable vector graphic. As exactly as is sounds these are scalable images that are great for responsive design. Being scalable is great because you can zoom on an SVG image and you will not get any degradation. Usually much smaller than a JPEG or a PNG. SVGs are written in XML code to produce the desired image. If you are not great with plotting points you could use Adobe creative suite to create one or an open source options are GIMP, Inkscape or Krita. Some other great use cases are they are great for creating a mask for images.

The History of SVGs

The history of SVGs go back to the early days of the internet. Created by the World Wide Wed Consortium (W3C) in the late 90s. It was developed to create a way for developers to have vector based imaged that could be scalable and allow for responsive design. Adoption of the svg was initially very slow but when realized by developers and designers how useful an svg can be it took off.

Parts of an SVG

An example of a SVG:

<svg viewBox='0 0 100 100'> <circle cx='50' cy='50' r='50' fill='red' />
</svg>

This will render a red circle, one of the basics shapes to make an without using a drawing application. The first part of the svg is viewBox, which defines min-x, min-y, width and height respectively. Moving the circle outside of the viewBox will cut that portion out. Since the drawing is completely scalable I find using 100 and using that a reference to percentages with plotting the rest of the numbers. Next is cx and cy, which is center x and center y plots for the circle. Then there is r which refers to radius of the circle. Finally, fill allows you to color with a few spelt out color and any hex code color.

How to make a SVG 

<svg viewBox='0 0 100 100'> <path stroke='white' fill='none' d=" M 10 10 H 90 V 90 H 10 V 10 " />
</svg>

Thought a complex looking element this is a simple white outline of a square. The most powerful properties for SVGs is path, anything can be drawn with it. The structure of the SVG path is followed by the d property that defines the path to be drawn. The structure for the drawing is defining the kind of line or the starting point which is defined by M. The numbers are followed by x, y plot points. H and V or horizontal and vertical points are followed by one number moving to the next plot point. The default fill is black setting a fill to none will make the area transparent. To find more properties I would suggest taking a look at the MDN Docs for the d properties such as drawing arcs and other lines/points.

Advance SVG Uses

Some cool tricks you can use SVG with is using the path property in your CSS to add changes to your SVG when you hover. An example of this would be:

index.html

<svg id="svg_css" viewBox="0 0 100 100"> <path fill="none" stroke="red" d=" M 100, 100 m -100, -50 a 50,50 0 1,0 100,0 a 50,50 0 1,0 -100,0 "/>
</svg>

stye.css

html, body { height: 100%; width: 100%;
}
#svg_css:hover path { d: path( "M 10,10 H 90 V 90 H 10 V 10" );
}

The HTML renders a red circle, and the CSS sets a rule on the hover to change the path of the SVG to a square. This is changing the path of the object but you could potentially change or add CSS rules the any of the properties including stroke or fill color.

Another very cool thing you can do is masking. Masking is filling the background of the SVG with an image. This effect can really add a new dimension to you SVGs.

<svg width="600" height="400"> <mask id="svgmask1"> <polygon fill="#ffffff" points="200 0, 400 400, 0 400" /> </mask> <image xlink:href="cat.jpeg" mask="url(#svgmask1)" />
</svg>

This will render a triangle with the cat.jpeg being masked out by the triangle. Potentially you could add multiple shapes by the and multiple images within one SVG. This has really deep and has a endless possibility of creativity.

Conclusion

This is just the basics of SVGs and techniques to use them to get a deeper understanding you should take a deeper dive into the MDN Docs on SVGs; there is so much more that delved into. There is such a wide set of use cases for SVGs that solve so many developer problems and open the door for being creative. For more complex images that you might want to develop I would suggest using am art application such as Inkscape or Gimp and it will convert your vector image into an SVG.
item.author: codingmustache

item.title: Lab10 反思 | Lab10 Reflect
item.description:
刘彤

发表于在这周，我将我的SSGifier上传到一个名为conan的 C++ 包管理器。 这周我学到了很多东西，试图了解 conan 是如何工作的，我如何将它集成到我的项目中，以及我如何将我的项目封装并上传到远程服务器以供其他用户使用。 但是conan只是提供包作为库供其他项目使用，不像python中的pip那样可以单独安装包使用。 也就是说，我们需要创建一个柯南项目来使用这个包。 为了确保我的说明易于阅读并涵盖所有需要的要求，我要求 Piotr 使用我的 README.md 中的唯一说明安装它，他后来发现他安装我的包没有任何困难。

我是怎么设置柯南的？

要设置柯南，需要遵循几个步骤：

  1. 首先安装 python，因为 conan 依赖 python 来运行它的脚本。
  2. 使用pip install conan安装柯南。
  3. 在SSGifier工程文件夹下，新建一个package文件夹，在package里面新建一个src文件夹，将SSGifier的所有源文件放到package package/src文件夹下。
  4. 在package文件夹下，使用$ conan new mypackage/1.0 -t生成一个package模板供我们使用。
  5. 编辑生成的conanfile.py ，将要打包的源文件添加到函数def package(self)中。 例如， self.copy("*.h", dst="src", src="src") 。
  6. 完成conanfile.py设置后，我们需要在这里注册一个帐户来托管我们的包。
  7. 按照说明将远程包源添加到柯南。
  8. 在package文件夹中，使用conan upload . -r put-your-remote-name-here --all 现在包已上传到远程服务器。

如何使用套餐？

您还可以在项目中将 SSGifier 作为库使用。 要使用它，您需要执行以下操作：

  1. 从这里下载并安装Conan ，然后从这里安装clang 。
  2. 在您的项目中创建一个conanfile.txt并将[requires]类别中的SSGifier/1.0.0添加到conanfile.txt 。 点击查看官方文档。 您可以使用此模板：
 [requires] SSGifier/1.0.0 [generators] cmake
  1. 编辑位置C:\Users\<your user name>\.conan conan 中的conan.conf文件并将revisions_enabled = 1添加到[general] 。
  2. 使用此命令将此远程存储库添加到柯南conan remote add tommy-conan-local https://tommyliu.jfrog.io/artifactory/api/conan/tommy-conan-local
  3. 使用此命令将凭据信息添加到此存储库conan user -p AKCp8nHPJwRCr2my24TiMaikQgQUW4e3xRbqsiGw45j9nPfPdLWEREYigcMLLW4pQtqg18n8b -r tommy-conan-local lt19930320@gmail.com 。
  4. 转到您在步骤 2 中创建的项目目录（确保 conaninfo.txt），使用 conan install 安装 SSGifier conan install . -r tommy-conan-local conan install . -r tommy-conan-local
  5. 转到C:\Users\<your user name>\.conan\data\SSGifier\1.0.0\_\_\package\3fb49604f9c2f729b85ba3115852006824e72cab\src找到源文件。
  6. 编译这些代码。 对于 gcc，使用g++ -std=c++17 main.cpp Utils.cpp ArgumentQueue.cpp FileProcessor.cpp MessagePrinter.cpp ，对于 clang，使用clang++ -std=c++17 main.cpp Utils.cpp ArgumentQueue.cpp FileProcessor.cpp MessagePrinter.cpp 。
  7. 编译完成后，会生成一个名为 a.exe 的a.exe二进制可执行文件。

皮尤什 | 编码力矩 - 一旦暂停，liutng 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，liutng 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，liutng 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 liutng 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 liutng 的操作：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 liutng 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Tong Liu

Posted on Nov 26

In this week, I uploaded my SSGifier to a C++ package manager named conan. It was quite a lot of learning I had this week in trying to understand how does conan work, how do I integrate it into my project, and how do I encapsulate and upload a package of my project to remote server for other users to use. However, conan only provides packages as libraries for other projects to use, it is not like pip in python where we can install packages and use it standalone. That is being said, we need to createa conan project to use the package. To make sure that my instruction is easy to read and covers all needed requirements, I asked Piotr to install it with the only instruction from my README.md, and he later found out he has no difficulty installing my package.

How did I set up conan?

To set up conan, there are few steps need to be followed:

  1. Install python first because conan relies on python to run its script.
  2. Use pip install conan to install conan.
  3. In the SSGifier project folder, create a folder named packageand create a folder src inside package and put all source files of SSGifier to the package/src folder.
  4. In package folder, use $ conan new mypackage/1.0 -t to generate a package template for us to use.
  5. Edit the generated conanfile.py and add the source files that to be packed into the package into the function def package(self). For example, self.copy("*.h", dst="src", src="src").
  6. After finished setting up the conanfile.py, we need to register an account here to host our packages.
  7. Follow the instruction to add the remote package source to conan.
  8. In the package folder, use conan upload . -r put-your-remote-name-here --all to upload the package to remote. Now the package is uploaded to the remote server.

How to use the package?

You can also use SSGifier in your projectas as a library. To use it you need to do following:

  1. Downlaod & install Conan from here and clang from here.
  2. Create a conanfile.txt inside your project and add SSGifier/1.0.0 in the category of [requires] to conanfile.txt. Click to see official documents. You can use this template: 
[requires]
SSGifier/1.0.0

[generators]
cmake
  1. Edit conan.conf file in the location C:\Users\<your user name>\.conan and add revisions_enabled = 1 to [general].
  2. Add this remote repository to conan by using this command conan remote add tommy-conan-local https://tommyliu.jfrog.io/artifactory/api/conan/tommy-conan-local
  3. Add credential information to this repository by using this command conan user -p AKCp8nHPJwRCr2my24TiMaikQgQUW4e3xRbqsiGw45j9nPfPdLWEREYigcMLLW4pQtqg18n8b -r tommy-conan-local lt19930320@gmail.com.
  4. Go to the directory of you project which you created in step 2(make sure conaninfo.txt), install SSGifier by using conan install . -r tommy-conan-local
  5. Go to C:\Users\<your user name>\.conan\data\SSGifier\1.0.0\_\_\package\3fb49604f9c2f729b85ba3115852006824e72cab\src to find the source file.
  6. Compile these code. For gcc, use g++ -std=c++17 main.cpp Utils.cpp ArgumentQueue.cpp FileProcessor.cpp MessagePrinter.cpp, for clang, use clang++ -std=c++17 main.cpp Utils.cpp ArgumentQueue.cpp FileProcessor.cpp MessagePrinter.cpp.
  7. After compilation, a binrary executatable of SSGifier will be generated with the name of a.exe.

Piyush | Coding Torque - Nov 25

约瑟夫·德布拉西奥

发表于前几天我正在编写一些 Python 代码来阅读有关 covid-19 的一些统计数据，在这样做的同时我遇到了一个面向开发人员的博客网站。 该站点有数百个关于编程、开发、云计算、框架以及与编写代码有关的任何内容的博客。

item.title: 对请求更改的恐慌！ | Panic on change of Request!
item.description:
需要程序员的速度每天都在变得越来越高。 但据说

如果我决定学习这两种语言，到2024年，另一种语言成为最需要的怎么办！。 现在的问题是

我希望我的这篇文章会激怒所有人：反 JavaScript 斗士们，对我们在现代网站上大量使用的东西感到震惊； 人们争论网络无论如何都是交互式应用程序的破平台，我们应该重新开始；

反应用户； 使用手工 JS 和手工编写的 HTML 的守旧派； 和 Tom MacWright，自从多年前我第一次知道他在 Mapbox 上的工作以来，我一直很钦佩他。 但我想这就是发表意见的代价。

The rate at which programmers are needed is becoming pretty much higher every day. But it is said that

JavaScript and Python are the most demanding of all..
what if I decided to learn both language and in 2024, another language becomes the most needed!. The question now is

I expect I'll annoy everyone with this post: the anti-JavaScript crusaders, justly aghast at how much of the stuff we slather onto modern websites; the people arguing the web is a broken platform for interactive applications anyway and we should start over;

React users; the old guard with their artisanal JS and hand authored HTML; and Tom MacWright, someone I've admired from afar since I first became aware of his work on Mapbox many years ago. But I guess that's the price of having opinions.
