item.title: The Rising Coder - 第 13/13 周（项目第 3/3 周） | The Rising Coder - Week 13/13 (Project Week 3/3)
item.description:
早上好，下午好，晚上好！ 它终于到来了，The Rising Coder 的最后一篇博客文章和我在Northcoders Bootcamp的旅程，这绝对是一种乐趣，我不会用这宝贵的 13 周经验来换取任何东西。

从支持我和同学的导师，到在过程中的每一步总是平易近人和支持我的同组成员，再到我的最终项目团队，他们在过去三周的过程中非常高兴与他们一起工作。

在整个训练营期间，我经常与以前的校友Atlas Sproson交谈，我是通过一个共同的 Discord 服务器偶然认识的，他的建议和话语在整个训练营期间一直伴随着我。 他告诉我，一旦时钟敲到下午 5 点，当你坐火车回家的路上，现实就开始了。你意识到你的旅程暂时结束了，虽然这是一个苦乐参半的离别，但绝对是永别了！

那么，我在 Northcoders 训练营的最后一周到底发生了什么，我未来的计划是什么？ 随意跳到下面指定的部分：

  • 如果你能匹配我 - 内部社交活动
  • 最后一周时间表
  • 最后一天过得怎么样
  • 自我反省
  • 未来的想法 - 现在怎么办？

如果你能匹配我 - 内部社交活动

由于 Northcoders 拥有庞大的招聘合作伙伴网络，这些合作伙伴有兴趣招聘 Northcoders 毕业生，因此 Northcoders 举办的活动之一称为“如果可以就匹配我”，缩写为 MMIYC。 该活动旨在将 Northcoders 毕业生与招聘合作伙伴联系起来，以便我们能够踏上我们的第一个初级软件工程师职位的大门。 老实说，我无论如何都不是交际花，当我报名参加这个活动时，我真的觉得自己超出了自己的深度和舒适区。

然而，进入这个训练营让我变得更好。 以前很容易忽略这个机会，或者因为紧张而完全取消我的出席。 但是，我从这次训练营中学到的是，如果你不愿意投入时间并将自己推出舒适区，那么你将无法为自己创造任何积极的变化。

因此，当我预订了从麦克尔斯菲尔德到曼彻斯特的 17.00 英镑往返机票时，我就下定决心要在 MMIYC 活动期间尽我最大的努力。 由于这些类型的事件和空间不是我喜欢的，我在几天前疯狂地研究了一些 YouTube 视频，以帮助自己在心理和身体上做好准备。

在此下方，我将链接三个视频，这些视频在我很长时间以来第一次参加社交活动时帮助了我，希望它能对您有所帮助。 但是，我的主要收获是，只要你为自己设定一个目标，并推动自己走出舒适区，给自己一个机会，那么你就会取得进步——所以尽你最大的努力，我相信你不会后悔它根本！

最后一周时间表

对于那些不熟悉 Northcoders 的最终项目阶段结构或者可能需要刷新的人，这里是细分：

  • 第 1 周 - 敏捷方法论介绍、团队建设练习和 Spiking 技术
  • 第 2 周 - 编写项目代码
  • 第 3 周 - 完善项目的 MVP（最小可行产品）并展示您的项目

在最后项目阶段的第二周，我们专门建立项目的 MVP，在第三周之后——我们的目标是在周三完全完成编码。 这并不是说我们完成了具有所有MVP 和后 MVP 功能的项目，而是要进入一个我们可以轻松展示项目的阶段。

星期四本身是为将在星期五使用的项目演示视频预留的。 由于每个队列中 Northcoder 的数量迅速增长，最近对最终项目阶段演示的结构进行了更改，因此我们的团队与我们队列中的其他团队有点混淆。

然而，这里是对最终项目阶段演示的期望的简要总结：

  • 必需- 您需要录制最多 3 分钟的带画外音的应用程序现场演示视频。 这将包括有关项目是什么、为项目探索了哪些技术堆栈以及您的项目具有哪些功能的信息。
  • 必需- 提交一个 Dropbox，其中包含您的项目的现场演示视频以及每个团队成员的画外音和照片。
  • 必需- 提交一个 Typeform，其中包括：您的团队名称、您团队的项目名称、您的团队使用的技术的广泛列表以及原因、您的团队成员的每个姓名以及将提交给 Northcoders Project Showcase 的每个团队的个人传记项目。
  • 可选- 如果您有时间，您将能够创建一个幻灯片放映，其中包含您的技术栈并简要说明，您认为项目进展顺利的地方，您和您的团队可以做得更好的地方，以及如果给予更多时间您和您的团队希望在未来实施什么。

最后一天如何进行

今天是我在 Northcoders 的最后一天，如果不去办公室就不算正式的告别！ 前两次来办公室是我上课的第一天（8月30日）和前两天（11月23日）参加MMIYC活动，那两次从Piccadilly Station到曼彻斯特城市大学附近的 Northcoders 办公室。

原因既是不住在曼彻斯特又经常去曼彻斯特，而且我的 iPhone 上的罗盘跟踪功能已关闭，所以我的手机无法正确跟踪我要去的地方——但我在前一天设法找到了这个所以我只花了 15 分钟就走到了那里！

吉姆（队列首席导师）给了我们一项名为“复古”或“回顾”的任务，开始了这一天。 这是一段很短的时间，你和你的团队聚在一起，私下在便利贴上写下你认为进展顺利的事情，哪些事情可以做得更好，以及你想在未来努力争取什么。 但这样做的诀窍是，你们还没有向对方展示你们所写的内容，这样你们和你们的团队成员都不会受到彼此的影响。 所以当揭晓的时候，你们都把它放在一块板上并讨论它。

或者……对我和我的团队来说就是这种情况。 由于我们的团队已经在前一天就以上所有点进行了讨论，我们已经受到了彼此的影响，因此实际的复古任务对我们来说有点困难😂

尽管如此，很高兴终于能够在办公室亲自见到我的团队，该团队由Ellie Lyons 、 Kirils Knazevs 、 Nikita Selanko 、 Antony Lester和Jon Linford组成（不幸的是，Antony 和 Jon 未能出席）。

快进到 11 点 30 分，演出终于要上路了！ 我们被转移到办公室的一个小会议室里，坐在一排排的座位上，盯着屏幕上的演示文稿，其中包括演示团队的名字和我们前一天提交的项目演示视频。

在我们的队列中创建了很多伟大的项目，其中包括：

  • 一款受 Wordle 启发的原创游戏，名为WordPearl
  • 一款名为All Aboard的互动应用程序，用于寻找朋友一起玩棋盘游戏
  • 一个允许用户跟踪他们的物品以防止他们丢失的应用程序称为wHere
  • 一个交互式应用程序，通过创建和加入固定在地图上的活动来查找本地活动，称为Activime
  • 一个用于未来待办事项的遗愿清单应用程序，具有内置的交流功能，可以互相激励，称为WhyNot
  • 一种适用于所有美食家的滑动式匹配系统，称为Dinder
  • 一个交互式应用程序，旨在教孩子们如何种植自己的植物，称为“幼苗”
  • 一个漂亮的小应用程序，可以找到你孩子的下一份圣诞礼物，叫做圣诞老人的小帮手
  • 一个专门用于跟踪来自多个英国超市的最新折扣的应用程序，称为Supermaaaart
  • 一款受 Pokemon-Go 启发的应用程序，可让您快速截取一只鸟的屏幕截图，以找出它的物种，称为Birdly
  • 一款鼓励您外出旅行的游戏化应用程序，名为Questr

然后是我们的最终项目Find 'n Dine - 将在下面介绍！

我们原来的 G3O 队名本来是要保持匿名让全世界看到的，但由于周四提交错误队名时的一些误传，我们被称为“Overlords”队，因为没有让任何人知道什么“Team G3O”真的很有意义🤦

除此之外，进入我们的最终项目 - 它到底是什么，我们为什么要构建它以及我们使用什么技术堆栈来创建它？

简而言之， Find 'n Dine是一个跨平台应用程序，旨在帮助优柔寡断的美食家通过手指轻扫找到他们下一个最喜欢的用餐地点。 用户将看到一副卡片，其中包含有关餐厅的 Google Review Stars、餐厅的网站和位置、图像轮播以及将餐厅添加到收藏夹列表的信息。

当用户决定向右滑动表示感兴趣，向左滑动表示不感兴趣时​​，他们会看到一个小的“Helper Icon”，其形式为绿色的心形和红色的破碎心形，分别用于左右滑动。

如果用户希望从三个列表（感兴趣/不感兴趣/最喜欢）中的任何一个中删除一家餐厅，那么他们只需在每张餐厅卡片上向左滑动即可删除，或者如果他们真的想清除整个列表则他们会单击“清除”按钮，这会从用户的本地存储中删除整个列表。

当我们在项目阶段的第一周讨论潜在的项目想法时，我们最初想到了这个想法，当时我们讨论了“我们面临什么问题，什么可以使它变得更容易？” 在此之前，我们的团队还有另外两个项目想法，其中一个项目想法是我们最初想要的，直到它被……枪杀了😂然后这个项目想法浮出水面，这就是我们到达的地方到项目阶段结束！

对于最终项目的前端和后端开发，我们使用了TypeScript ，它是 JavaScript 的类型安全超集，因为我们最初已经对学习 TypeScript 感兴趣，因为我们经常在市场上听到它，但它直到我们自己开始使用它时，我们才了解它的好处以及它在多大程度上改善了开发人员的体验。

事不宜迟，我将向您介绍我们用于前端和后端的技术堆栈：

前端技术栈

  • 然而，由于 React-Native 没有预捆绑包来让开发人员的工作更轻松，我们选择使用 Expo Go，它允许我们在手机上实时查看应用程序的更改，并且能够不要像传统 React-Native 中要求的那样编写一行原生 iOS/Android 代码。
  • 最初我们尝试使用传统的 React-Native 异步存储，但是当我们使用 Expo Go 时——这个库还没有得到支持，因此我们必须找到一个变通方法。 幸运的是，我们发现 Redux 允许我们将所有与用户餐厅列表相关的状态存储在单个商店中，并且通过使用 Redux-Persist，我们能够持久保存这些数据，这样它就不会在重新加载时丢失。

后端技术栈

  • 因此，每个对象都具有彼此不同且完全非关系的不一致数据，因此我们选择使用 MongoDB，因为它具有非关系数据库的性质并且能够相对轻松地存储 JSON 对象. 一个简单的 TLDR 是，除非指定，否则 REST API 通常会返回 JSON 对象上的所有数据，因此您可能会因此获取不足/过度获取。 然而，GraphQL 消除了这一点，因为你只需要获取你需要的数据。 为了让我们的团队从数据库中检索数据，我们使用 Apollo GraphQL 和 Mongoose 轻松连接两者以快速开始进行查询。

自我反省

正如我在这篇博文开头提到的，当我告别 Northcoders 导师和其他队列成员，然后最后是我的团队成员时 - 我突然意识到一切都结束了，就像那样......我不会'不要撒谎说分道扬镳并不难过，但我真的很高兴我加入了Northcoders 训练营并遇到了我在课程中遇到的每一个人。

从课程开始时完全焦虑的崩溃到很长一段时间以来最快乐的感觉，这是一个昼夜的差异。 回顾自从开课以来我取得了多大的进步，以及去年我在精神上并不处于最佳状态的那一年，这真是太棒了。 我已经能够了解如此多的热门技术，包括 JavaScript、TypeScript、Node、Express、GraphQL、Redux、Tailwind 等等，我终于可以自信地说我可以无限期地增加价值，我绝对以前不能说。

进入训练营本身，我想做出贡献并创造一种安全的氛围，鼓励彼此积极友好地对待彼此，Northcoders 工作人员和我的同事们都给予了十倍的回报！

最初我开始写这个博客是因为我想记录我自己的旅程，这样我就可以反思我所做的事情并可能为未来的 Northcoders 提供指导，但是每当队列中的人第一次见到我并打招呼时并且他们已经阅读了我的博客，老实说，我非常高兴！ 所以如果你正在阅读这篇文章并且你是他们中的一员，那么感谢你阅读和支持我，我真的祝你一切顺利，即使我们终于完成了现在的旅程并毕业了！！！😌

前进的想法

虽然这是一次旅程的结束，但不代表我的旅程就此结束！ 回到家后，终于感受到我在 Northcoders 的时间结束并在写这篇博文之前迷失了现实，我意识到我真的很高兴能够向自己和其他感兴趣的人表达我一直在做的事情我可以鼓励别人。

所以我的想法是，我想创建一个每周博客系列，其中包含我那一周所做的事情，这样我就可以保持稳定的自我反省，这样我就可以跟踪我所做的事情完成了，让你们了解我所做的一切😊

也许如果您作为同龄人正在阅读这篇文章，并且认为开始写您自己的经历和旅程为时已晚，那么我真的恳请您自己尝试一下！ 当你提到你已经准备好我的博客文章时，有额外的人在背后给你额外的推动，就像你们为我所做的那样确实有帮助，所以如果你决定开始写你自己的博客条目，那么请标记我我一定会和你一起踏上你的旅程！ 但就目前而言，在回到我的工作之前我肯定会休息一下，再次感谢您一路上的所有持续支持，我希望在我的下一个系列中见到您，该系列也将出现在我的博客页面上！

社交

请随时关注我下面的任何社交活动！



原文:

Good morning, good afternoon and good evening! It has finally arrived, the final blog entry to The Rising Coder and my journey at the Northcoders Bootcamp, and what an absolute pleasure it has been and I would not trade in this precious 13-week experience for anything at all.

From the mentors that supported myself and fellow students, my fellow cohort members that were always approachable and supportive during every step of the way, and to my final project team that were an absolute pleasure to work with over the course of the past three weeks.

Throughout the bootcamp I was regularly talking with a previous alumni Atlas Sproson who I coincidentally met through a mutual Discord server, and his advice & words stuck with me throughout the bootcamp. He had told me that once the clock hits 5PM and you're on your way back home on the train that the reality begins to set in. You realise that your journey has ended for now, and whilst it was a bittersweet parting, it's definitely not goodbye forever!

So, what exactly happened during my final week at the Northcoders Bootcamp and what are my future plans going forward? Feel free to skip ahead to the specified sections below at your pleasure:

  • Match Me If You Can - Internal Networking Event
  • Final Week Schedule
  • How the final day went
  • Team G3O's Final Project
  • Self-Reflection
  • Thoughts going forward - what now?

Match Me If You Can - Internal Networking Event

As Northcoders has a large network of hiring partners that are interested in hiring Northcoders Graduates, one of the events that Northcoders holds is called the "Match Me If You Can" that is abbreviated to MMIYC. This event aims to connect Northcoders Graduates with hiring partners so that we can get our foot in the door with our first Junior Software Engineer positions. I will be completely honest with you, I'm not the social butterfly by any means and I really felt out of my depth and comfort zone when I signed up for this event.

However, going into this bootcamp has changed me for the better. Previously it would have been easy to ignore this opportunity or to cancel my attendance altogether because of nerves. But, what I had learnt from my time at this bootcamp is that if you aren't willing to put in the time and push yourself out of your comfort zone then you won't be able to create any positive changes for yourself.

So the moment I booked my £17.00 return ticket from Macclesfield to Manchester, I made a stern committment to try my best during the MMIYC event. As these types of events and spaces are not what I'm comfortable with, I frantically researched a few days prior for a few YouTube videos to help myself feel prepared both mentally and physically.

Below this I will link the three videos that helped me out during my first time in a long time attending a networking event in hopes that it will help you. But, my main takeaway is that as long as you set yourself a goal and you push yourself of your comfort zone to give yourself a chance, then you will make progress - so give it your best shot, I'm sure you will not regret it at all!

Final Week Schedule

For those that are unfamiliar with the final project phase structure at Northcoders or perhaps need a refresh, here's the breakdown:

  • Week 1 - Introduction to Agile Methodology & Team-building exercises & Spiking Technology
  • Week 2 - Coding your project
  • Week 3 - Polishing off your project's MVP (Minimum Viable Product) & Presenting your project

During the second week of the final project phase, we exclusively build up our project's MVP, and following the third week - we aim to completely finish coding altogether on Wednesday. This is not to say that we finish the project entirely with all MVP and post-MVP features, but to get to a stage that we are comfortable demonstrating the project.

Thursday itself is reserved for the project's demonstration video that would be used for Friday. Our team had a bit of confusion alongside other teams in our cohort because of recent changes to the structure of the final project phase demonstrations due to the numbers of Northcoders rapidly growing each cohort.

However, here is a brief summary of what to expect for the final project phase demonstration:

  • REQUIRED - You will need to record a live demonstration video of your application for a maximum of 3-minutes with a voiceover. This will include information regarding what the project is, what tech stack was explored for the project, and what features your project has.
  • REQUIRED - Submitting a Dropbox that includes your project's live demonstration video with voiceover & photos of each team member.
  • REQUIRED - Submitting a Typeform that includes: Your team name, your team's project name, an extensive list of technology your team used and why, each name of your team member with personal biographies that would be submitted to the Northcoders Project Showcase for each individual team's projects.
  • OPTIONAL - If you have time, you will be able to create a slide show that has your tech-stack and explained in brief detail, what you think went well for your project, what you and your team could have done better, and if given more time what would you and your team have liked to implement in the future.

How The Final Day Went

Today was my final day at Northcoders and it wouldn't have been a proper farewell without going to the office! The previous two times I came to the office were my first day of the course (August 30th), and two days ago (November 23rd) for the MMIYC event, and those two times it took me 35 minutes to get from Piccadilly Station to the Northcoders Office which is near Manchester Metropolitan University.

The reason for that is both a mix of not living in Manchester and visiting often, and my compass tracking on my iPhone being turned off so my phone didn't properly track where I was going - but I managed to find this out a day before so it only took me 15 minutes to walk there as it should!

We started the day off by being given a task by Jim (The cohort lead mentor) called the "Retro" or "Retrospective". This is in short a brief period where you and your team get together and write on sticky notes in private about the things that you think went well, what could have gone better, and what you want to strive for in the future. But the trick to this is that you don't show each other what you have written yet so that neither of you and your team members are influenced by one another. So when the time comes for the reveal, you all put it on a board and talk through it all.

Or... That would have been the case for me and my team. As our team had already talked through the day prior about all of the above points, we had already been influenced by one-another and so the actual retro task for us was a bit difficult 😂

Although besides that, it was very nice being able to finally meet my team that comprises of Ellie Lyons, Kirils Knazevs, Nikita Selanko, Antony Lester andJon Linford in person at the office (Antony & Jon were unfortunately not able to come though).

Fast-forwarding to 11:30, it was finally to get the show on the road! We were moved into one of the small conference rooms in the office and sat in rows of seats staring at the screen that housed the presentation that included the presenting team's name & project demonstration video that we submitted a day prior.

There were so many great projects that were created during our cohort that included:

  • An original Wordle-inspired game called WordPearl
  • An interactive application to find friends to play boardgames with called All Aboard
  • An application that allows users to track their belongings so they never get lost called wHere
  • An interactive application to find local activities through creating & joining activities pinned on the map called Activime
  • A bucketlist application for future to-do activities with built-in communication features to inspire each other called WhyNot
  • A swipe-powered matching system for all of the foodies called Dinder
  • An interactive application aimed to teach children about growing your own plants called Seedlings
  • A nifty little application to find your kid's next Christmas present called Santa's Little Helper
  • A built-for-purpose application to track the latest discounts from multiple UK supermarkets called Supermaaaart
  • A Pokemon-Go-inspired application that enables you to take a quick screenshot of a bird to find out its species called Birdly
  • A gamified application that incentivises you to journey outside called Questr

And then there was our final project called Find 'n Dine - which will be covered below!

Team G3O's Final Project - Find 'n Dine

Our original name of Team G3O was meant to remain anonymous for the world to see, but because of a few miscommunications when submitting the incorrect team name on Thursday, we were outed as team "Overlords", so much for not letting anyone know about what "Team G3O" really meant 🤦

Besides that, getting into our final project - what exactly is it, why did we build it and what tech stack did we use to create it?

Find 'n Dine - Brief Summary

So quite briefly, Find 'n Dine is a cross-platform application that was built to help indecisive foodies find their next favourite dining location with a swipe of a finger. The user will be presented with a deck of cards that contains information regarding the restaurant's Google Review Stars, the restaurant's website & location, a carousel of images and the ability to add a restaurant to a list of favourites.

When the user decides to swipe right for interested and left for not interested, they are shown a little "Helper Icon" in the form of a green heart and red broken-heart for respective right and left swipes.

If the user wishes to remove a restaurant from any of the three lists (Interested/Not Interested/Favourited), then they just need to swipe to the left on each restaurant card to delete, or if they really want to clear the entire list then they would click the Clear button that would erase the entire list from the user's local storage.

The idea initially came across when we were discussing potential project ideas during the first week of the project phase when we had discussions of "What problems do we face and what could make it easier?" - and the topic that came up was "Trouble picking something new for date night", and that was when the seed was planted.

Prior to this our group had two other project ideas and of which one of those project ideas was what we originally gunned for until it was... gunned down 😂 And then this project idea arose to the surface and so that is where we have arrived to by the end of the project phase!

Find 'n Dine - Tech Stack

For both the Front-End and Back-End development of our final project, we used TypeScript which is a type-safe superset of JavaScript because we were already initially interested in learning about TypeScript because we constantly hear about it in the market, but it was only until we started using it ourselves that we understood the benefits and how much it improves developer experience.

Without further ado, I will introduce you to the tech stack that we used for both the Front-End and Back-End:

Front-End Tech Stack

  • React-Native & Expo Go - As our team wanted to create a cross-platform application that runs on both iOS and Android, we opted to use React-Native because of the cross-platform development nature of the platform and the accessible transition. However, as React-Native does not come with a pre-bundled packages to make lives of developers any easier, we opted to use Expo Go which allows us to view the changes to our application on our phones live, as well as being able to not write a single line of native iOS/Android code like you may be required to in traditional React-Native.
  • Redux & Redux-Persist - Our vision for the application was to ensure that the user's list of restaurants were to be stored locally and not be deleted upon reloading the application. Initially we tried to use the traditional React-Native Async Storage, however as we were using Expo Go - this library was yet to be supported and as a result, we had to find a work-aroound. Luckily we found Redux that allowed us to store all of this state pertaining to the restaurant lists of the user in a single store, and through using Redux-Persist, we were able to persist this data so that it was not lost on reloads.

Back-End Tech Stack

  • MongoDB & Mongoose - The dataset that we were working with originally included Antony Lester having to web-scrape for over 6+ hours on the Friday of the first week of the project phase, to which the number of lines of code was well over 260,000+. As a result of this, each of the objects had inconsistent data that was different from each other and were completely non-relational, and so we opted to use MongoDB because of it's non-relational database nature and ability to store JSON objects with relative ease. Mongoose was used to easily connect up our API server and host online.
  • GraphQL & Apollo GraphQL - Similar to our reasoning behind learning a new piece of technology, we had heard about GraphQL as an alternative to the traditional REST API. A quick TLDR is that REST APIs will typically return all of the data on a JSON object unless specified and so you could be under/over-fetching as a result. However, GraphQL eliminates this because you only fetch for the data you need. In order for our team to retrieve data from the database, we used Apollo GraphQL with Mongoose to easily connect the two to start making queries quickly.

Self-Reflection

As I mentioned at the beginning of this blog post, when I bid my farewells to the Northcoders tutors and fellow cohort members, and then finally my team members - it dawned on me that it's all over, just like that... I won't lie and say that it wasn't sad to part ways, but I really am glad that I joined the Northcoders Bootcamp and met everyone I had on the course.

From being a completely anxious wreck at the start of the course to feeling the happiest I have been in a long while, it's a night-and-day difference. Reflecting on how much progress I have made since both the start of the precourse and last year where I was not in the best place mentally, it has been amazing. I've been able to learn about so much in-demand technologies including JavaScript, TypeScript, Node, Express, GraphQL, Redux, Tailwind and so much more and I can finally confidently say that I can indefinitely add value, to of which I definitely could not have said previously.

Going into the bootcamp itself I wanted to contribute and create a safe atmosphere that encourages one-another to be positive and friendly towards one-another, and both the Northcoders Staff and my fellow cohort members reciprocated tenfold!

Originally I started writing this blog because I wanted to document my own journey so I can reflect on what I've done and perhaps give guidance to future Northcoders, but whenever people on the cohort meet me for the first time and drop in to say hi and that they've read my blog, I'm honestly beyond happy! So if you are reading this and you were one of them, then thank you for reading and supporting me, I really wish you all the best even after we have finally finished our journey for now and graduated!!!😌

Thoughts Going Forward

Although this is the end of one journey does not mean it's the end of my journey! After arriving home and finally feeling the reality of my time at Northcoders ending and laying lost before writing this blog post, I realised that I really enjoyed being able to voice to myself and others who were interested, about what I've been doing in hopes that I can encourage others.

So my thoughts going forward are that I would like to create a weekly blog series that will contain things that I have done for that week so that I can keep a solid routine of self-reflection so that I can keep track of what I've done, and keep you guys in the loop of what I've been up to 😊

Perhaps if you are reading this as a fellow cohort member and thought it was too late to start writing about your own experiences and journey, then I really implore you to give it a try yourself! Having that extra somebody to give you that extra nudge on the back like you guys have done for me when you mention you've ready my blog posts really does help, and so if you decide to start writing your own blog entries then please tag me and I'll be sure to join you on your journey together! 😊

But for now, I will definitely be taking a small break before returning to my grind, and again thank you for all of the continued support along the way and I hope to see you in my next series that will also be on my blog page!

Socials

Feel free to give me a follow on any of my socials down below!
item.author: clam119

item.title: Arquitetura: Monolíticos, Monolíticos Distribuídos e Microsserviços | Arquitetura: Monolíticos, Monolíticos Distribuídos e Microsserviços
item.description:
简介

最重要的时刻是软件架构的依赖者做上下文相关的设备，这些设备是集成的必要设备，是复杂架构应用程序的注册设备。 desenvolvimento mais trabalhoso。
正极桥

  • 微型服务建筑

    阿尔塔disponibilidade;

中音；

  • 阿尔塔复杂;
  • 结论

    Portanto, pode-se concluir que por meio da explicação breve dada a cima, sem aprofundamento, a escolha da arquitetura vai variar de acordo com diferentes trade-off's do atual contexto da empresa ea necessidade da regra de negócios. Portanto, pode-se concluir que por meio da explicação breve dada a cima, sem aprofundamento, a escolha da arquitetura vai variar de acordo com diferentes trade-off's do atual contexto da empresa ea necessidade da regra de negocios。 Por conseguinte, não existe bala de prata , cada caso é um, e deve ser estudado e analisado da melhor forma pela equipe. Por conseguinte, não existe bala de prata , cada caso é um, e deve ser estudado e analisado da melhor forma pela equipe。



    • 原文:

    Nesse post iremos falar um pouco sobre os tipos de arquitetura, cada caso de uso e seus pontos positivos e negativos de acordo com diferentes conjunturas.

    Introdução

    A primeiro momento vale relembrar que a escolha do tipo de arquitetura de software irá depender do contexto em que a equipe de desenvolvimento está inserida, se a equipe possui o conhecimento necessário para aplicar regras de complexidade arquitetural e se a regra de negócios necessite de um tipo de desenvolvimento mais trabalhoso.
    Sendo assim, pode-se dizer que a escolha pelo tipo de arquitetura irá depender de múltiplas variáveis, e é responsável do arquiteto determinar qual a melhor escolha a ser feita de acordo com cada peso e complexidade dos requisitos de negócio.

    Arquitetura Monolítica

    De modo geral, arquitetura monolítica é aquela em que toda aplicação roda em único processo, de forma que cada componente estão ligados a um único programa. Apesar da dependência gerada por esse tipo de arquitetura serem amenizados com técnicas de desenvolvimento, como a Inversão de Controle e/ou Injeção de Dependência, ele sofre de rigidez, mesmo que fraca. Isso acontece pelo fato do sistema ficar disponível ou indisponível por completo, tendo em vista que todas suas implementações estarem inseridas no mesmo processo.

    É um tipo arquitetural em que há a necessidade de desenvolvimento de uma aplicação em que necessite uma facilidade de gerenciamento e menor complexidade de código, nas quais os seus serviços não necessite funcionar de maneira isolada.

    Mas como saber se meus serviços precisam funcionar de maneira isolada?

    Para saber se seus serviços necessite funcionar de maneira isolada, primeiro é necessário definir quais são suas responsabilidade, sua complexidade, e qual atuação ele vai ter perante o sistema. Por outro lado, a depender da regra de negócio, seus serviços terão que funcionar de maneira isolada quando a disponibilidade (capacidade de um serviço continuar funcionando perante o mal funcionamento dos outros, 24 horas/7 dias na semana) for mais necessária que a consistência.

    Vale ressaltar, que arquitetura monolítica é caracterizada por diferentes áreas/controllers da aplicação terem acesso a um mesmo banco de dados, como representado na imagem abaixo:

    O grande problema, é que em regras de negócios que necessitem de um processamento denso de informações, como o levantamento de relatórios, o banco de dados pode sofrer de gargalos, de modo a afetar outros componentes da aplicação que necessite acesso ao mesmo banco de dados.

    Normalmente, as arquiteturas monolíticas sofrem de Memory Leak's.

    Pontos Positivos

    • Facilidade de gerenciamento do código (A primeiro momento);
    • Menor complexidade de código;
    • Menor custo/Menos uso de recursos tecnológicos;
    • Necessidade de menor conhecimento técnico;

    Pontos Negativos

    • Dificuldade de gerenciamento do código (A longo prazo);
    • Acoplamento entre as partes do sistema;
    • Riscos de 100% de indisponibilidade de acesso;
    • Dificuldade em escalar horizontalmente;

    Arquitetura de Monolítico Distribuído

    De modo geral, arquitetura monolítica distríbuida é aquela em que a aplicação roda em diferentes processos, no entanto, seu banco de dados está unificado, ou seja, diferentes processos/serviços acessam o mesmo ponto de armazenamento de dados. Como na imagem a seguir:

    Esse tipo de arquitetura está sujeita a inconsistência de acesso a dados e performance, haja vista que enquanto um serviço está realizando trocas de dados com o banco, outro também estará, podendo um afetar o outro, havendo sobre carregamento ou até mesmo bug's por acesso paralelo.

    Por outro lado, esse tipo de arquitetura não abstrai o conhecimento que um serviço tem sobre o outro, tendo em vista que eles são interligados pelos dados armazenados no banco.

    Sendo assim, pode-se dizer que o monolítico distribuído se aproveita da complexidade dos microsserviços, mas não se aproveita da abstração das relações de cada processo.

    Pontos Positivos:

    • Disponibilidade de serviço (em caso de queda de outros componentes);

    Pontos Negativos:

    • Perda de rastreabilidade de inconsistência de dados;
    • Inconsistência de dados;
    • Sobre carregamento de banco de dados em contextos de alto processamento de dados;
    • Complexidade de um microserviço.

    Arquitetura em Microsserviços

    De modo geral, arquitetura monolítica distríbuida é aquela em que a aplicação roda em diferentes processos, em que cada processo possui sua própria complexidade e acessa sua própria fonte de dados. Como na imagem a seguir:

    Desse modo, com cada complexidade de funcionalidade possuindo seu próprio serviço, a disponibilidade, flexibilidade e rastreabilidade de cada um é potencializada positivamente.

    Pontos positivos:

    • Problemas de funcionamento com hospedagem de dados e de aplicação fica apenas no próprio serviço;
    • Baixo custo para manutenção (a longo prazo);
    • Facilidade no desenvolvimento de novas funcionalidades;
    • Facilidade em escalar de acordo com a necessidade (serviço mais sobrecarregado é escalado mais vezes);
    • Alta disponibilidade;

    Pontos negativos:

    • Alto custo;
    • Alta complexidade;
    • Alto custo para manutenção (a primeiro momento);
    • Inconsistência de dados;

    Conclusão

    Portanto, pode-se concluir que por meio da explicação breve dada a cima, sem aprofundamento, a escolha da arquitetura vai variar de acordo com diferentes trade-off's do atual contexto da empresa e a necessidade da regra de negócios. Por conseguinte, não existe bala de prata, cada caso é um, e deve ser estudado e analisado da melhor forma pela equipe.
item.author: otaviovb

item.title: 如何从 Android 模拟器截取屏幕截图？ | How to Take Screenshot from Android Emulator?
item.description:
您可以从 Android Emulator 控件菜单或 Android Studio Logcat 菜单截取屏幕截图

我太愚蠢了，我一直在从 Android 模拟器手动截取屏幕截图（使用 Windows Snipping Tool ），而不知道 Android Studio 中已经有“屏幕捕获”功能。

以下是从 Android 模拟器截取屏幕截图的 2 种方法：

单击相机图标

你可以用框架捕捉

或没有框架

单击保存并选择要保存的位置。

安卓工作室日志

您可以在 Android Studio Logcat 中执行相同的操作

它带出相同的窗口，除了它有这个“通用电话”或“通用平板电脑”可供选择。

正如您在上面看到的，该框架是一个通用框架，与您的模拟器中的实际框架略有不同。 这是此方法的唯一区别。 还有额外的“向左旋转”和“向右旋转”按钮，您可以通过模拟器控件轻松实现这些按钮。

结论

老实说，为什么要提供不止一种方法来做同样的事情？ 这是我对Android开发的总体感受。 我们总是有不止一种方法来做同一件事，在所有方面，包括编码和实现。

最初发布于https://vtsen.hashnode.dev 。

一旦暂停，vtsen 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，vtsen 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，vtsen 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 vtsen 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 vtsen：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 vtsen 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

You can take screenshot either from the Android Emulator controls menu or Android Studio Logcat menu

I was so stupid, I have been taking screenshot manually (using the Windows Snipping Tool) from Android emulator without knowing there is already "Screen Capture" feature in Android Studio.

These are 2 ways to take screenshot from your Android Emulator:

Android Emulator Controls Menu

Click on the camera icon

You can capture with frame

or without frame

Click Save and select the location where you want to save.

Android Studio Logcat

You can do the same in Android Studio Logcat

It brings out the same window, except it has this "Generic Phone" or "Generic Tablet" to choose from.

As you can see above, the frame is a generic frame which is slightly different from the actual frame in your emulator. That's the only difference in this method. There are also additional Rotate Left and Rotate Right buttons, which you can so do it easily from the emulator controls.

Conclusion

Honestly, why give more than one way to do a same thing? This is my general feeling on Android development. We always have more than one way to do a same thing in all aspects, including coding and implementation.

Originally published at https://vtsen.hashnode.dev.

Elliot Brenya sarfo - Nov 17

Once suspended, vtsen will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, vtsen will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by vtsen will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If vtsen is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag vtsen:

vtsen consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging vtsen will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: vtsen

item.title: AWS Community Builders 计划如何让 re:Invent 2022 感觉像“回家”，即使我自己去了那里 | How the AWS Community Builders program made re:Invent 2022 feel like “coming home” even tho I went there on my own
item.description:
但不仅如此，今年的 AWS re:Invent 对我来说更像是“回家”而不是“去参加会议”。

让我解释一下为什么：

在那之后，我发生了很多变化，我想与您分享其中的一些！

我加入了社区建设者计划，并因此获得了很好的交流机会、会议、演讲和活动以及一些不公开的信息。 接下来，我向一群人介绍了自己，并迅速与全球其他 Builders 进行了互动和联系。 我看到了 CDK 日的论文征集 (CfP)，我们在 2022 年 CDK 日与几位建设者——Danielle 、 Saima 、 Christian和Matt——就“本地云”进行了精彩的小组讨论。之后我参加了 AWS 峰会Berlin，并亲自认识了 AWS 社区 DACH 的许多优秀人士（仅举几位：Linda、Markus、Thorsten、Aaron、Stefan、Nora、Henning ……）。 这次峰会让我明白了社区工作对我来说有多么重要，以及我从谈话和社交中获益多少——re:Meet，正如克里斯蒂安最近所说的那样。

我一直很享受与很多建设者的对话，对他们中的很多人有了更好的了解。 今年晚些时候，我启动了“AWS 用户组 Bergstraße”，我们开始定期举行聚会。 我还加入了“AWS 社区日 DACH”组织小组，并帮助建立了 AWS DACH 社区的“AWS 社区支持组织”……并能够在德累斯顿举行的 2022 年 AWS 社区日上发表演讲。 我遇到了更多 AWS 社区的优秀成员，亲自了解他们并与他们共度时光。

作为社区建设者计划的一部分，在 re:Invent 2022 上还有一个用于会谈和会议的 CfP——我提交了四个会谈，正如我已经提到的，我很幸运，其中一个被选为今年 re:Invent 的 DevChat .

那时我决定亲自参加 re:Invent，以便有机会发表演讲并分享我的经验。 我不知道我公司的任何人或亲密的朋友都不会和我一起去拉斯维加斯。 我期待着带上一些亲密的朋友和同事。 相反，我正在前往 re:Invent，而我的团队或组织中没有其他人参加。

尽管如此，我还是要回家了，我觉得这将是我参加过的最好的 re:Invent 大会。

我将遇到很多我以前从未见过的社区建设者——即使在今天的航班上，也有一些我认识的“来自社区”的人以及我对 AWS 的其他投资（Tobias、Oliver、Henning 等）。 周日，我们将与 10 多位我以前从未见过的建设者一起举办有史以来第一次 pre:Invent 社区建设者远足活动。 之后，我们将与 20 多位建设者会面，参加自行组织的晚宴活动。

然后，在星期一，会议将开始，我会觉得自己是 Werner 几年前在他的主题演讲中谈到的“大型 AWS 构建者家族”的一员。

整个星期都充满了会议、一对一、会议——还有晚餐、派对……

这些东西让我在拉斯维加斯有宾至如归的感觉！

期待与大家见面并交谈、学习和享受乐趣。 如果您想“现场”见我，请联系我。 我非常感谢能成为这个伟大社区的一员。



原文:

re:Invent 2022 is about to start in Las Vegas and I am really looking forward to all of the sessions, the gamified learning possibilities and all of the other things that make the conference great. But more than that, AWS re:Invent this year feels for me like “coming home” instead of “going to a conference”.

Let me explain you why:

In march 2022 I received my acceptance to the AWS Community Builders program.

 After that a lot of things changed for me and I would like to share some of them with you!

I got added to the Community Builders program and with that I gained access to great networking opportunities, sessions, talks and events and also some information that are not publicly available. Next, I introduced myself to a bunch of folks and people and quickly interacted and connected with other Builders around the globe. I saw the Call for Papers (CfP) for CDK day and we had a great panel discussion with a few builders – Danielle, Saima, Christian and Matt – about “The local cloud” at CDK Day 2022. Afterwards I attended the AWS Summit in Berlin and got to know a lot of great people of the AWS Community DACH in person (just to mention a few: Linda, Markus, Thorsten,  Aaron, Stefan, Nora, Henning…). This summit made me understand how important community work is for me and how much I gain from talking and networking – re:Meet, as Christian recently said.

I kept enjoying conversations with a lot of builders, getting to know a lot of them better. Later in the year, I kicked off the “AWS UserGroup Bergstraße” and we started having regular meetups. I also joined the “AWS Community Day DACH” organizational group and helped to found the “AWS Community Support Organization” for the AWS DACH community…and was able to give a presentation at the AWS Community Day 2022 in Dresden. I met more great members of the AWS community, got to know them in person and spend time with them.

As part of the Community Builders program there also was a CfP for talks & sessions at re:Invent 2022 – I submitted four talks and as I already mentioned I was fortunate and one of them has been selected as a DevChat for this year’s re:Invent.

At that time I decided that I would be attending re:Invent in person, to get the chance to give the talk and share my experiences. I did not know that no one from my company or close friends would be joining me in Las Vegas. I was expecting to take along a few of my close friends and colleagues. Instead, I’m on my way to re:Invent and no one else from my team or organization is attending.

And still, I’m coming home and I have  the feeling its going to be the best re:Invent I have ever attended.

I’ll be meeting a lot of Community Builders I have never seen before – even on the flight today there were a few people I knew “from the community” and from my other investments into AWS (Tobias, Oliver, Henning, …). On Sunday, we’re going to be doing the ever-first pre:Invent Community Builders Hiking event with more than 10 builders I’ve never met before. Afterwards, we will be meeting up with more than 20 builders for a self-organized dinner event.

And then, on Monday, the conference will start where, I will feel like being part of the “big AWS builders family” that Werner was talking about at his keynote a few years ago.

The whole week is filled with meetings, 1on1, sessions – and dinners, parties,…

These things make me feel at home in Las Vegas!

Looking forward to meet all of you in person and talk, learn and have fun. Reach out if you want to meet me “live”. 😊

I can’t be more thankful to be part of this great community.
item.author: lockhead

item.title: 了解独立组件和模块化组件 | Understanding Standalone Component and Modularized Component
item.description:
嘿伙计们，你们好吗！？

今天我就来说说什么是standalone components和component with module，它们有什么区别，我们又该如何实现呢！

那么，让我们开始吧！

让我们看看代码如何：

要生成独立组件，我们可以运行如下命令，我们只需要使用--standalone标志：

 👇🏻 Generating the component: ng generate component myStandAloneComponent --standalone

要在另一个组件中使用我们的独立组件，我们只需要将组件作为“模块”导入到我们的模块中，在下面的代码中，我们将组件导入到HomeModule中：

 import { MyStandAloneComponentComponent } from ' ../../my-stand-alone-component/my-stand-alone-component.component ' ; @ NgModule ({ declarations : [ HomeComponent , ], imports : [ CommonModule , HomeRoutingModule , MyStandAloneComponentComponent ] })

你可以使用正常的组件，例如：

 <hello name= "{{ name }}" ></hello> <app-standalone></app-standalone>

如何在独立组件中使用其他组件：

我们可以在独立组件中使用其他组件、指令或任何其他内容，因为我们需要在my-stand-alone-component. component.ts中的@Component({})中导入模块。 这里我们有实时代码示例：

在我看来，当使用模块化时，我们可以更好地组织其组件依赖性，更好地管理延迟加载。 该模块“像一个盒子”，在盒子里我们只放我们需要的东西，例如：

我有一个横幅组件，在它里面我们需要图像组件，所以我们只需要导入ImageModule就可以使用图像了。

当我们的组件中有module时，我们有一个文件来声明、导入和导出我们需要的，也就是说，我们有一个文件来分隔我们的导入和我们用来创建的my-component.component.ts中我们的模块、输入、输出或其他任何东西，现在你！？ 在我看来，这种“模块化”很棒，也适合组织。

现在，让我们创建一个模块化组件！

现在，让我们使用 Angular CLI 创建我们的组件并使用--skip-import因为我们将创建我们的模块并在新模块中声明该组件：

 👇🏻 Generating the component: ng generate component myStandAloneComponent --skip-import

现在，我们将创建模块：

 👇🏻 Generating the component module: ng generate module myStandAloneComponent

之后要在其他组件中使用该组件，我们需要在我们的模块中声明和导出我们的组件，因此在我们生成的新模块中我们需要这样做，看看下面的代码：

 ... import { MyComponentComponent } from ' ./my-component.component ' ; @ NgModule ({ imports : [ CommonModule ], declarations : [ MyComponentComponent ], exports : [ MyComponentComponent ] })

现在要使用我们可以正常使用的组件，就像独立组件一样，但是我们需要在我们要使用的组件的模块中导入MyComponentModule ，看一下：

模块代码：

 import { MyComponentModule } from ' ./components/my-component/my-component.module ' ; @ NgModule ({ imports : [ BrowserModule , FormsModule , MyComponentModule ], declarations : [ AppComponent , HelloComponent ], bootstrap : [ AppComponent ], }) export class AppModule {}

这里我们也有实时代码示例：

在我看来，模块化更好、更有条理、更健壮！

对你来说，你更喜欢在哪一个上工作？

就这些啦，希望大家喜欢，有什么问题，建议或者其他话题，欢迎评论！

回头见✌🏼



原文:

Hey guys, how are you!?

Today I'll talk about what is standalone components and components with module, what is the difference between their and how we can implement it!

So, let's start!

🧩 Standalone component:

Standalone is an optional property to use and isn't necessary to use with component, we can use it with Directive too, and when we create a component or directive like standalone we don't need to be declared it in a @NgModule, so we don't need to stay tuned with the our module organization! Let's see how is the code:

🎯 First: Generate the standalone component:

To generate a standalone component we can run the command like below, we just need to use the --standalone flag:

👇🏻 Generating the component:
ng generate component myStandAloneComponent --standalone

🎯 Second: To use the standalone component:

To use our standalone component in another component we just need to import the component in our module, as a " module", in the code below we import the component in HomeModule:

import { MyStandAloneComponentComponent
} from '../../my-stand-alone-component/my-stand-alone-component.component'; @NgModule({ declarations: [ HomeComponent, ], imports: [ CommonModule, HomeRoutingModule, MyStandAloneComponentComponent ]
})

And you can use the component normal, for example:

<hello name="{{ name }}"></hello>
<app-standalone></app-standalone>

How to use other's component's in a standalone component:

We can use other components, directives or anything else inside our standalone component, for that we need to import the modules in our @Component({}) inside your my-stand-alone-component. component.ts, as below we are using the mat-spinner component, have a look:

import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common';
import { Component, NgModule, OnInit } from '@angular/core';
import { MatProgressSpinnerModule } from '@angular/material/progress-spinner'; @Component({ selector: 'app-standalone', standalone: true, imports: [MatProgressSpinnerModule], template: ` <div> <h2> I'm a standalone Component and I'm work! </h2> <mat-spinner></mat-spinner> </div> `, styles: [ ` h2 { color: #180D5B; } `, ],
})
export class StandaloneComponent implements OnInit { constructor() {} ngOnInit() {}
}

And here we have the live code example:

📦 Modularized component:

In my opinion when using modularized we have a better organization of its component dependencies, a better management over lazy loading. The module is "like a box" and inside the box we put only what we need, for example:

I have a banner component and inside it we need the image component, so we just import the ImageModule and we can use the image.

When we have the module in our component, we have a file to declare, import and export that we need, that is, we have a file to just separate our imports and inside our my-component.component.ts we just use to create our modules, inputs, outputs or anything else, now you!? In my opinion this "modularization" is wonderful and also for organization.

Now, let's create a modularized component!

🎯 First: Generate the component:

Now, let's create our component with Angular CLI and let's to use the --skip-import because we are will to create our module and declare the component inside the new module:

👇🏻 Generating the component:
ng generate component myStandAloneComponent --skip-import

Now, we'll to create the module:

👇🏻 Generating the component module:
ng generate module myStandAloneComponent

After that to use the component inside other components we need to declare and export our component in our module, so inside the new module we generated we need to do this, take a look at the code below:

...
import { MyComponentComponent } from './my-component.component'; @NgModule({ imports: [ CommonModule ], declarations: [ MyComponentComponent ], exports: [ MyComponentComponent ]
})

And now to use the component we can use it normally, like the standalone component but we need to import MyComponentModule inside the module of the component we want to use, take a look:

The module code:

import { MyComponentModule } from './components/my-component/my-component.module'; @NgModule({ imports: [BrowserModule, FormsModule, MyComponentModule], declarations: [AppComponent, HelloComponent], bootstrap: [AppComponent],
})
export class AppModule {}

The html code:

<hello name="{{ name }}"></hello>
<app-my-component></app-my-component>

And here we have the live code example too:

Modularization in my opinion is better, more organized and robust!

And for you, which one do you prefer to work on?

That's all guys, I hope you like it and if any questions, suggestions or other topics, please comment!

See you later ✌🏼
item.author: renancferro

item.title: React JS中如何使用Timer进行计算 | How to use Timer for calculation in React JS
item.description:
希西家02

发表于我正在做一个 React 项目，我需要系统进行计算，例如，如果用户提供的 Bomb 值为 10，Energy 为 5。这意味着系统每次生成的炸弹数量为 10 5 次（能量数）中的一分钟等于 50...所以，每隔一分钟我需要系统继续做加法，即一分钟内产生的炸弹数量为 10，两分钟产生的炸弹是20，三分钟产生的炸弹数量是30，依此类推直到五分钟（这是提供的能量值）之后

五分钟计时器将停止。 任何帮助请伙计们！

hezekiah02

I was working on a React project that I need the system to do a calculation e.g If the user supply a value of Bomb to be 10 and Energy to be 5. it means the number of bomb the system is going to generate is 10 at every one minute in 5 times(the number of energy) which is equal to 50... so, at every one one minute I need the system to keep doing the addition i.e The number of bomb generated at one minute is 10, the number of bomb generated at two minute is 20, the number of bomb generated at three minute is 30 e.t.c until five minute(which is the value of energy supplied) and after that

five minute the timer will stop. Any help on this please guys!

Mihai-Adrian Andrei - Nov 9

item.title: # 版本 0.2 - PR #1 | # Release 0.2 - PR #1
item.description:
发表于问题 #104中描述了一个问题，即面朝上翻转的图块不会以恒定大小呈现。 因此，如果图块使用不同尺寸的图像文件，则图块将在面朝上翻转时随意重新排列。

准备修复

准备修复不需要太多设置。 网络应用程序无法正确加载 CSS 存在问题。 我已经熟悉 CSS，所以只需分叉存储库，然后检查 Web 应用程序的 CSS 即可找到问题的根源。

然而，问题最终与我最初的预期大相径庭。 最初，我希望我必须编辑 Web 应用程序的样式表，但事实证明错误是由index.html生成的，而不是在样式表中。

为了进行修复，您需要学习什么？

最初，我希望我需要提高与设置元素及其内容的尺寸相关的 CSS 语法知识才能解决此问题。 然而，最后我只需要让自己熟悉哪些文件路径格式对于大多数处理 HTML 代码的浏览器来说是“有效的”。

代码说明

最初，在第一次检查 Basic-memory-game 网络应用程序的样式表时，我似乎无法在处理图像尺寸的 CSS 中找到语法错​​误。 因此，我决定尝试注释掉所有 CSS 并逐渐重新启用 CSS 代码的每一部分，直到其中一个破坏了程序。

尝试这样做后，我意识到根本没有渲染任何 CSS。

我检查了index.html文件并注意到为链接样式表文件的<link>元素提供了无效的文件路径。

但是，当 Web 应用程序由 VSC 的实时服务器扩展之外的任何浏览器呈现时，CSS 将完全中断。 我调整了href属性的值，在正斜杠 ( / ) 之前添加了句点 ( . )，CSS 开始正确呈现。 现在，所有显示在图块中的图像都以相同的大小呈现。

因此，问题在于，尝试链接样式表文件时提供的文件路径"/styles.css"被解释为当前工作目录中名为"styles.css"的文件夹，而不是.css文件名称为“样式” 。 结果，由于技术上不存在链接到index.html文件的样式表，因此没有 CSS 应用于 HTML。 调整文件路径的语法解决了这个问题。

代码演示

修复前

开始新游戏：

单击带有大字符图像的图块后：

修复后

开始新游戏：

单击带有大字符图像的图块后：

研究

我花了一些时间浏览网络，尤其是在线开发人员社区论坛，搜索讨论与网络浏览器和 HTML 相关的文件路径有效性的文章或线程。 我还没有找到具体说明./filename vs /filename的这个问题的东西，尽管我通过自己的一些测试对差异有了更好的理解。

因此，我得出了上述结论（在代码解释中）：

  • 字符串"/example"表示当前工作目录中名为“example”的文件夹。
  • 字符串"./example"和"example"是表示当前工作目录中名为“example”的文件的有效方式。

与项目维护者的互动

我与项目维护者没有明显的互动。

困难

解决这个问题最困难的部分是试图找到我认为是 CSS 中的语法错误的地方，但事实证明这是不存在的。

在意识到错误出在样式表链接中后，只用了几分钟就解决了这个问题。

拉取请求链接

基本记忆游戏PR #105

艾哈迈德·阿卜杜拉·埃尔比拉尔 - 一旦暂停，aparris21 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，aparris21 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aparris21 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aparris21 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 aparris21 的方法：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 aparris21 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

a-parris21

Posted on Nov 25

The Basic-memory-game repo hosts the code for a tile-matching memory game. There was a problem, described in Issue #104 where tiles that were flipped face-up would not be rendered at a constant size. Thus, if tiles used image files with different dimensions, then the tiles would be re-arranged haphazardly upon being flipped face-up.

Preparation for the fix

There was not much setup required in order to prepare the fix. It was a problem with the web app not loading the CSS correctly. I am already familiar with CSS, so it was just a matter of forking the repo and then inspecting the web app's CSS to locate the source of the problem.

However, the problem wound up being very different from my initial expectations. Originally, I expected that I would have to edit the stylesheets for the web app but it turned out the bug was generated by the index.html, and not within the stylesheets.

What did you need to learn in order to make the fix?

Originally, I expected that I would need to improve my knowledge of CSS syntax in relation to setting the dimensions for elements and their contents in order to resolve this issue. However, in the end I simply needed to familiarize myself with what filepath formats are considered 'valid' for most browsers that process HTML code.

Code Explanation

Originally, upon first inspecting the stylesheets for the Basic-memory-game web app, I could not seem to find a syntax error within the CSS that handled the dimensions of the images. Therefore, I decided to try commenting-out all of the CSS and gradually re-enabling each section of CSS code until one of them broke the program.

Upon attempting this, I realized that none of the CSS was being rendered at all.

I inspected the index.html file and noticed that there was an invalid filepath provided to the <link> element which linked the stylesheet file.

The value of the <link> element's href attribute was set to "/styles.css" which, worked correctly and rendered all the CSS appropriately when testing the app via VSC's live-server extension.

However, when the web app was rendered by any browser outside of VSC's live-server extension, the CSS would break completely. I adjusted the value of the href attribute, adding a period (.) before the forward-slash (/) and the CSS began rendering correctly. Now all of the images displayed in tiles were rendered at the same size.

Thus, the problem was that, the filepath provided when attempting to link the stylesheet file, "/styles.css", was interpreted as a folder in the current working directory with the name "styles.css", instead of a .css file with the name "styles". As a result, since the stylesheet linked to the index.html file technically did not exist, no CSS was applied to the HTML. Adjusting the filepath's syntax fixed this problem.

Code Demo

Before the fix

Start of a new game:

After clicking a tile with a large character image:

After the fix

Start of a new game:

After clicking a tile with a large character image:

Research

I spent some time browsing the web, especially online developer community forums, search for articles or threads discussing filepath validity in relation to web-browsers and HTML. I have yet to find something that speaks specifically to this problem with ./filename vs /filename, although I have gained a better understanding of the difference through some testing of my own.

As such, I arrived at the conclusion described above (in the Code Explanation):

  • The string "/example" repesents a folder named "example" in the current working directory.
  • The strings "./example" and "example" are the valid ways to represent a file named "example" in the current working directory.

Interactions with project maintainers

I had no notable interactions with the project maintainers.

Difficulties

The most difficult part in resolving this issue was attempting to locate what I believed to be a syntax error in the CSS, which turned out to be non-existent.

Upon realizing that the error was in the stylesheet linking, fixing the issue took just a few minutes.

Link to Pull Request

Basic-memory-game PR #105

Ahmed Abdalla Elbilal - Nov 19

Samina Rahman Purba - Nov 20

item.title: 代号随想录day11 | 20.Valid Parentheses, 1047. Remove All Adjacent Duplicates In String, 150. Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation 20. 有效括号，1047。删除字符串中所有相邻的重复项，150。评估反向波兰表示法 | 代码随想录day11 | 20.Valid Parentheses, 1047. Remove All Adjacent Duplicates In String, 150. Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
item.description:
给定一个包例如 '('，')'，'{'，'}'，'['，']' 的字符串，判断字符串是否有效。有效字符串：左括号左括号相同类型类型。必须以正确的的顺序闭合。注意注意符串符串可被被被认为认为是

输入: "()"输出: true输入: "()[]{}"输出: true输入: "(]"输出: false输入: "([)]"输出: false输入: "{[]}"输出: true

今日学习的文章链接

解题：开开个个个个这个这个这个中右括号们右括号们右括号们右括号们右括号们右括号们思路是是输入输入输入输入输入输入输入输入一旦一旦一旦碰上左括号，就就就把把其其其对应右括号入栈栈栈栈．

 //👍 use stack var isValid = function (s) { let stack = []; for (let char of s) { // 碰到左括号，给栈放右括号switch (char) { case '(': stack.push(')'); break; case '{': stack.push('}'); break; case '[': stack.push(']'); break; //碰到s中的右括号时，如果不匹配stack pop元素 则就不匹配, 直接返回false default: if (char !== stack.pop()) return false; } } //stack中要有对于元素的话，说明s中有多余的左括号或者右括号return stack.length === 0; };

s，s，s，重复重复会两且相同相同的的删除操作后返回最后的字符串。答案保证唯一一

示例：输入："abbaca" 输出："ca"解释：例如，在 "abbaca" 中，我们可以删除 "bb" 由于两字母相邻且相同，这是此时唯一可以执行删除操作的重复项。之后我们得到字符串 "aaca"，其中又只有 "aa" 可以执行重复项删除操作，所以最后的字符串为 "ca"。提示： 1 <= S.length <= 20000 S 仅由小写英文字母组成。

今日学习的文章链接

解题思路：打开一个栈，这个栈中维保护没有相邻重复项的所有字符串。这个维护方法：遍历string，当前面的char如果等于stack的最后一项，则开始消消乐，直接stack.pop；当前面的char如果不等于堆栈的最后一项，则将char压入堆栈。

 //👍 use stack: var removeDuplicates = function (s) { let stack = []; for (let char of s) { if (char !== stack[stack.length - 1]) { stack.push(char); } else { stack.pop(); } } return stack.join(''); };

根据法法， - 包括包括 +， - ， *， /。。个运算运算对象整数整数整数整数部分。 给定逆波兰表达式总是有效的。换句话说，表达式总会得到有效率值而不存在除数为0的情况。

示例 1：输入: ["2", "1", "+", "3", " * "]输出: 9解释: 该算式转化为常见的中缀算术表达式为：((2 + 1) * 3) = 9示例 2：输入: ["4", "13", "5", "/", "+"]输出: 6解释: 该算式转化为常见的中缀算术表达式为：(4 + (13 / 5)) = 6示例 3：输入: ["10", "6", "9", "3", "+", "-11", " * ", "/", " * ", "17", "+", "5", "+"]输出: 22解释:该算式转化为常见的中缀算术表达式为： ((10 * (6 / ((9 + 3) * -11))) + 17) + 5 = ((10 * (6 / (12 * -11))) + 17) + 5 = ((10 * (6 / -132)) + 17) + 5 = ((10 * 0) + 17) + 5 = (0 + 17) + 5 = 17 + 5 = 22逆波兰表达式：是一种后缀表达式，所谓后缀就是指算符写在后面。平常使用的算式则是一种中缀表达式，如 ( 1 + 2 ) * ( 3 + 4 ) 。该算式的逆波兰表达式写法为 ( ( 1 2 + ) ( 3 4 + ) * ) 。逆波兰表达式主要有以下两个优点： 1. 去掉括号后表达式无歧义，上式即便写成 1 2 + 3 4 + * 也可以依据次序计算出正确结果。 2. 适合用栈操作运算：遇到数字则入栈；遇到算符则取出栈顶两个数字进行计算，并将结果压入栈中

今日学习的文章链接

解题思路：打开一个栈，这个栈里只允许出现数字并维护计算结果。怎么维护呢？遍历遍历？？？？？一旦就就栈栈遍历结绳。

 //👍 use stack: var evalRPN = function (tokens) { let stack = []; let right, left; for (let char of tokens) { switch (char) { case '+': stack.push(stack.pop() + stack.pop()); break; case '-': right = stack.pop(); left = stack.pop(); stack.push(left - right); //要左边减右边break; case '*': stack.push(stack.pop() * stack.pop()); break; case '/': right = stack.pop(); left = stack.pop(); stack.push((left / right) | 0); //要左边除右边break; default: // when char is number sting, so convert to number and pushed into stack stack.push(parseInt(char)); } } return stack[0]; };

学习总结：

  1. 碰到类似消消乐的题目，大部分使用stack解决
  2. 使用stack时，先想清楚stack中维保持的是什么，再去想维护stack的规则等。最后明确输出的结果和stack有什么关系

Implement Stack using Queues 10 代码随想录day11 | 20.Valid Parentheses, 1047. Remove All Adjacent Duplicates In String, 150. Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation

给定一个只包括 '('，')'，'{'，'}'，'['，']' 的字符串，判断字符串是否有效. 有效字符串需满足： 左括号必须用相同类型的右括号闭合。 左括号必须以正确的顺序闭合。 注意空字符串可被认为是有效字符串。

输入: "()"
输出: true

输入: "()[]{}"
输出: true

输入: "(]"
输出: false

输入: "([)]"
输出: false

输入: "{[]}"
输出: true

今日学习的文章链接

解题思路：开一个stack, 在这个stack中维持右括号们,具体思路是遍历输入string,一旦碰上左括号，就把其对应的右括号入栈，一旦碰上右括号就将其和stack.pop元素对比，如果不一样就是括号不匹配。直接return false。 重复以上动作直到结束遍历string, 最后看stack的长度为不为0。 为0代表Valid Parentheses.

//👍 use stack

var isValid = function (s) {
  let stack = [];

  for (let char of s) {
    // 碰到左括号，给栈放右括号
    switch (char) {
      case '(':
        stack.push(')');
        break;
      case '{':
        stack.push('}');
        break;
      case '[':
        stack.push(']');
        break;
      //碰到s中的右括号时，如果不匹配stack pop元素 则就不匹配, 直接返回false
      default:
        if (char !== stack.pop()) return false;
    }
  }

  //stack中要有对于元素的话，说明s中有多余的左括号或者右括号
  return stack.length === 0;
};

leetcode 1047 - Remove All Adjacent Duplicates In String

给出由小写字母组成的字符串 S，重复项删除操作会选择两个相邻且相同的字母，并删除它们。在 S 上反复执行重复项删除操作，直到无法继续删除。在完成所有重复项删除操作后返回最终的字符串。答案保证唯一

示例：
输入："abbaca"  输出："ca"
解释：例如，在 "abbaca" 中，我们可以删除 "bb" 由于两字母相邻且相同，这是此时唯一可以执行删除操作的重复项。之后我们得到字符串 "aaca"，其中又只有 "aa" 可以执行重复项删除操作，所以最后的字符串为 "ca"。

提示：
1 <= S.length <= 20000
S 仅由小写英文字母组成。

今日学习的文章链接

解题思路：开一个stack, 这个stack中维护all string without ajacent duplocates. 这么个维护法: 遍历string, 当前char如果等于stack的最后一项，则开始消消乐，stack.pop directly; 当前char如果不等于stack的最后一项，则push char into stack.

//👍 use stack:

var removeDuplicates = function (s) {
  let stack = [];

  for (let char of s) {
    if (char !== stack[stack.length - 1]) {
      stack.push(char);
    } else {
      stack.pop();
    }
  }

  return stack.join('');
};

leetcode 150 - Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation

根据 逆波兰表示法，求表达式的值。有效的运算符包括 + , - , * , / 。每个运算对象可以是整数，也可以是另一个逆波兰表达式。 说明：整数除法只保留整数部分。 给定逆波兰表达式总是有效的。换句话说，表达式总会得出有效数值且不存在除数为 0 的情况。

示例 1：
输入: ["2", "1", "+", "3", " * "]
输出: 9
解释: 该算式转化为常见的中缀算术表达式为：((2 + 1) * 3) = 9

示例 2：
输入: ["4", "13", "5", "/", "+"]
输出: 6
解释: 该算式转化为常见的中缀算术表达式为：(4 + (13 / 5)) = 6

示例 3：
输入: ["10", "6", "9", "3", "+", "-11", " * ", "/", " * ", "17", "+", "5", "+"]
输出: 22
解释:该算式转化为常见的中缀算术表达式为：
((10 * (6 / ((9 + 3) * -11))) + 17) + 5       
= ((10 * (6 / (12 * -11))) + 17) + 5       
= ((10 * (6 / -132)) + 17) + 5     
= ((10 * 0) + 17) + 5     
= (0 + 17) + 5    
= 17 + 5    
= 22 

逆波兰表达式：是一种后缀表达式，所谓后缀就是指算符写在后面。
平常使用的算式则是一种中缀表达式，如 ( 1 + 2 ) * ( 3 + 4 ) 。
该算式的逆波兰表达式写法为 ( ( 1 2 + ) ( 3 4 + ) * ) 。
逆波兰表达式主要有以下两个优点：
1. 去掉括号后表达式无歧义，上式即便写成 1 2 + 3 4 + * 也可以依据次序计算出正确结果。
2. 适合用栈操作运算：遇到数字则入栈；遇到算符则取出栈顶两个数字进行计算，并将结果压入栈中

今日学习的文章链接

解题思路：开一个stack，这个stack里只允许出现number并维护计算结果. 怎么维护呢？遍历tokens，一旦碰到string number就入栈，如果碰到运算符号则开始对最好两个数进行计算，并且把计算好的值入栈来维持最新的计算结果，持续以上直到遍历结束.

//👍 use stack:

var evalRPN = function (tokens) {
  let stack = [];
  let right, left;

  for (let char of tokens) {
    switch (char) {
      case '+':
        stack.push(stack.pop() + stack.pop());
        break;
      case '-':
        right = stack.pop();
        left = stack.pop();
        stack.push(left - right); //要左边减右边
        break;
      case '*':
        stack.push(stack.pop() * stack.pop());
        break;
      case '/':
        right = stack.pop();
        left = stack.pop();
        stack.push((left / right) | 0); //要左边除右边
        break;
      default: // when char is number sting, so convert to number and pushed into stack
        stack.push(parseInt(char));
    }
  }

  return stack[0];
};

学习总结：

  1. 碰到类似消消乐的题目，大多使用stack解决
  2. 使用stack时，先想清楚stack中维持的是什么， 再后来去想维护stack的rules etc. 最后明确输出的结果和stack什么关系
